sentences.zh: 最近我在东北的时候和人们发生了激烈的争论，一个人说他们“讨厌Waymo”并且拒绝详细说明（但也说他们每天都在使用AI工作），另一个人则直截了当地说技术是邪恶的，所以我问他们穷人是否应该没有电。

sentences.en: Then they restricted their argument to AI is evil, and that was a more nuanced (if not trite) debate, which they eventually conceded.
sentences.zh: 然后他们将他们的论点限制在人工智能是邪恶的，这是一个更微妙（如果不是陈词滥调）的争论，他们最终承认了这一点。

sentences.en: But not without some gnawing and thrashing!
sentences.zh: 但并非没有一些啃咬和殴打！

blocks.html_en: But I don't think that hating AI or technology or whatever to be contrary to Trump is a genuine political statement, its just reaction. It involves politics in the same way that everything we do involves politics, but it is not "consciously" political in the same way that a baby demanding care from an overworked mother is also not consciously political. Baby wants milk, your average liberal wants to feel self-righteous (they would honestly do better with the milk). Someone can claim anything they want is political, it does not mean they are doing it for genuinely political reasons, they are probably just doing it because they fear the censure of their friends and the dissolution of their social life. Not engaging with something because you are afraid of how it could harm you personally, consciously or no, is not an objective way of making a decision that effects society more broadly. Like, a baby will just die if it doesn't get milk, you won't necessarily die because some of your friends stop talking to you.

sentences.en: But I don't think that hating AI or technology or whatever to be contrary to Trump is a genuine political statement, its just reaction.
sentences.zh: 但我不认为憎恨人工智能或技术或任何与特朗普相反的东西是真正的政治声明，它只是反应。

sentences.en: It involves politics in the same way that everything we do involves politics, but it is not "consciously" political in the same way that a baby demanding care from an overworked mother is also not consciously political.
sentences.zh: 它涉及政治，就像我们所做的一切都涉及政治一样，但它不是“有意识地”政治，就像一个婴儿要求过度劳累的母亲照顾也没有有意识地政治一样。

sentences.en: Baby wants milk, your average liberal wants to feel self-righteous (they would honestly do better with the milk).
sentences.zh: 婴儿想要牛奶，一般的自由主义者想要感到自以为是（老实说，他们会用牛奶做得更好）。

sentences.en: Someone can claim anything they want is political, it does not mean they are doing it for genuinely political reasons, they are probably just doing it because they fear the censure of their friends and the dissolution of their social life.
sentences.zh: 有人可以声称他们想要的任何事情都是政治的，但这并不意味着他们这样做是出于真正的政治原因，他们可能只是因为害怕朋友的谴责和社交生活的解体而这样做。

sentences.en: Not engaging with something because you are afraid of how it could harm you personally, consciously or no, is not an objective way of making a decision that effects society more broadly.
sentences.zh: 因为担心某件事可能会有意或无意地伤害你个人而不参与某件事，这并不是做出影响更广泛社会的决策的客观方式。

sentences.en: Like, a baby will just die if it doesn't get milk, you won't necessarily die because some of your friends stop talking to you.
sentences.zh: 就像，如果婴儿得不到牛奶就会死，你也不一定会因为你的一些朋友不再和你说话而死。

blocks.html_en:
The article is a fun read. There's a certain amount of hyperbole and subtext which may perhaps be unfamiliar to some readers.

But the point is fair. Where are the huge AI wins? Why haven't Anthropic and OpenAI produced a regular series of case studies where Company X did Fun AI Thing and incredible success ensued?

Why do these conversations always end up mentioning chat bots?

Why do c-suites seem to be full of people chasing trends instead of leading?

Does anyone remember what customers are, or are corporations aiming for Platonic abstraction, where business activities become ends in themselves, with no useful output?

sentences.en: The article is a fun read.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章读起来很有趣。

sentences.en: There's a certain amount of hyperbole and subtext which may perhaps be unfamiliar to some readers.But the point is fair.
sentences.zh: 其中有一定数量的夸张和潜台词，可能对某些读者来说是陌生的。但要点是公平的。

sentences.en: Where are the huge AI wins?
sentences.zh: 人工智能的巨大胜利在哪里？

sentences.en: Why haven't Anthropic and OpenAI produced a regular series of case studies where Company X did Fun AI Thing and incredible success ensued?Why do these conversations always end up mentioning chat bots?Why do c-suites seem to be full of people chasing trends instead of leading?Does anyone remember what customers are, or are corporations aiming for Platonic abstraction, where business activities become ends in themselves, with no useful output?
sentences.zh: 为什么 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 没有定期制作一系列案例研究，让 X 公司做了有趣的 AI 事情并取得了令人难以置信的成功？为什么这些对话最终总是提到聊天机器人？为什么高管层似乎充满了追逐趋势而不是引领潮流的人？还有人记得客户是什么吗？或者公司是否正在追求柏拉图式的抽象，在这种抽象中，业务活动本身就成为目的，没有任何有用的输出？

blocks.html_en:
The prominent "Business Owners" link on this company's website is broken. "Not Found".

Perhaps this will get downvoted but I personally take with a grain of salt anything written/stated by a company that can't even get the most basic functions (like running a simple website) correct.

And the even bigger irony here is that the author has a ranty blog post in which he claims he saved his employer $500,000 by clicking a button. "[It] is fucking wild that an inefficiency that took me five minutes to solve in a GUI configuration panel was allowed to persist," he wrote.

https://ludic.mataroa.blog/blog/i-accidentally-saved-half-a-...

blocks.html_en:
This is an interesting strategy for trying to impress potential customers.

Most consultants try to impress you by talking about the great companies they've worked for.

This blog post screams, "Look how dumb my past coworkers were!" from top to bottom, then expects us to be impressed with their experience?

sentences.en: This is an interesting strategy for trying to impress potential customers.Most consultants try to impress you by talking about the great companies they've worked for.This blog post screams, "Look how dumb my past coworkers were!"
sentences.zh: 这是一个试图给潜在客户留下深刻印象的有趣策略。大多数顾问试图通过谈论他们曾工作过的伟大公司来给你留下深刻印象。这篇博客文章尖叫着，“看看我过去的同事有多愚蠢！”

sentences.en: from top to bottom, then expects us to be impressed with their experience?
sentences.zh: 从上到下，然后期望我们对他们的经历印象深刻？

blocks.html_en:
I don't think this is surprising at all. I'm pretty sure I've saved my employers similar amounts in the past, because what happens is something gets configured months or years ago, the dev leaves, everyone forgets and assumes things are how they are.

I've had to delete some really silly code that would slow things down or just force waits as a result of either dealing with legacy APIs or some other arcane reason. Not without testing or making sure it wasn't there for a reason mind you, but these inefficiencies can sometimes be hidden big problems.

sentences.en: I don't think this is surprising at all.

blocks.html_en:
I don't think this is surprising at all. I'm pretty sure I've saved my employers similar amounts in the past, because what happens is something gets configured months or years ago, the dev leaves, everyone forgets and assumes things are how they are.

I've had to delete some really silly code that would slow things down or just force waits as a result of either dealing with legacy APIs or some other arcane reason. Not without testing or making sure it wasn't there for a reason mind you, but these inefficiencies can sometimes be hidden big problems.

blocks.html_en:
> And who hasn't seen productivity gains from more established AI technology

You have to be very careful about claiming productivity gains. There may have been some instances of gains in a specific part of a workflow but does it slow down others or result in overall gains is yet to be empirically measured and validated. We’re seeing metrics like more lines of code, better unit tests, documentation, faster PRs etc. but the actual gains of businesses are still a question mark. Do more PRs lead to faster features being shipped or does it lead to slower reviews or bug ridden code that breaks user experience? I’ve see a lot of companies tout their metrics around more code being shipped but the same companies aren’t talking about how that translates to an actual dollar amount.

sentences.en: > And who hasn't seen productivity gains from more established AI technologyYou have to be very careful about claiming productivity gains.
sentences.zh: > 谁还没有看到更成熟的人工智能技术带来的生产力提升？在声称生产力提升时必须非常小心。

sentences.en: There may have been some instances of gains in a specific part of a workflow but does it slow down others or result in overall gains is yet to be empirically measured and validated.
sentences.zh: 工作流程的特定部分可能有一些收益的实例，但它是否会减慢其他部分的速度或导致整体收益尚待凭经验测量和验证。

sentences.en: We’re seeing metrics like more lines of code, better unit tests, documentation, faster PRs etc. but the actual gains of businesses are still a question mark.
sentences.zh: 我们看到了更多的代码行、更好的单元测试、文档、更快的 PR 等指标，但企业的实际收益仍然是一个问号。

sentences.en: Do more PRs lead to faster features being shipped or does it lead to slower reviews or bug ridden code that breaks user experience?
sentences.zh: 更多的 PR 是否会导致更快的功能发布，或者是否会导致审查速度变慢或代码充满错误而破坏用户体验？

sentences.en: I’ve see a lot of companies tout their metrics around more code being shipped but the same companies aren’t talking about how that translates to an actual dollar amount.
sentences.zh: 我看到很多公司都在宣传他们的衡量标准是关于交付更多代码，但这些公司却没有谈论如何将其转化为实际的美元金额。

blocks.html_en:
These things have been "good" for a while now. And yet, companies like Amazon and Microsoft aren't showing significant improvement in their most visible products.

If it's not obviously showing for the all-in, AI-selling companies, I simply don't expect serious improvement for everyone else.

They're undeniably neat tools, but so far there's no observable evidence that they're transformative.

blocks.html_en:
I think there's a big difference between individual employees using AI tools to boost their productivity - with things like Claude Code and Codex - and "AI projects" where companies build custom software on top of LLMs.

The former is easy to get right. Any software engineer (at least provided they aren't actively resisting the technology )can get useful results out of Claude Code these days.

The latter is really hard. LLMs are a strange beast to build software on, and most of the obvious projects - like the internal chatbots described in this article - are easy to have over-promise and under-deliver.

blocks.html_en: I get what you are saying, and while I'll say it is definitely not 0%, I have seen very little in the way of useful software that is primarily generated. The vast majority just does not go the distance for whatever reason. I could explain many reasons, but I am getting really tired of explaining myself. If the tools were as great as everyone says, we'd be going through a software Renaissance, but we're not. I would argue a software dark ages since it feels like things are getting worse and I find bugs in what was historically very long running and stable applications. But, whatever. I think the author is clearly talking about modern AI, I don't think they need to be explicit about models.

sentences.zh: 绝大多数人只是因为某种原因而没有走这么远的路。

sentences.zh: 我可以解释很多原因，但我真的厌倦了解释自己。

sentences.en: I would argue a software dark ages since it feels like things are getting worse and I find bugs in what was historically very long running and stable applications.
sentences.zh: 我认为软件进入了黑暗时代，因为感觉事情正在变得更糟，而且我在历史上运行时间非常长且稳定的应用程序中发现了错误。

sentences.en: But, whatever.
sentences.zh: 但是，无论如何。

sentences.en: I think the author is clearly talking about modern AI, I don't think they need to be explicit about models.
sentences.zh: 我认为作者显然在谈论现代人工智能，我认为他们不需要明确模型。

blocks.html_en: I get what you are saying, and while I'll say it is definitely not 0%, I have seen very little in the way of useful software that is primarily generated. The vast majority just does not go the distance for whatever reason. I could explain many reasons, but I am getting really tired of explaining myself. If the tools were as great as everyone says, we'd be going through a software Renaissance, but we're not. I would argue a software dark ages since it feels like things are getting worse and I find bugs in what was historically very long running and stable applications. But, whatever. I think the author is clearly talking about modern AI, I don't think they need to be explicit about models.

blocks.html_en:
Look, if they have data and it says 0%, and you have vibes that say that can’t be true, who should we believe?

Do you work with lots of companies and see large AI success stories?

Or do you just vibe that you personally find AI useful so it must also be a business success?

Look, I honestly don’t care, but I think “it must be false” is also unsubstantiated hyperbole. If an agency says they see no AI success, I see no particular reason to believe they’re lying.

They’re not saying AI can’t be a success. They’re saying they haven’t seen it. That matches my experience too. Proven AI success stories seem… vague, when you dig into the details, in my personal experience.

It doesn’t seem surprising to me.

sentences.en: Look, if they have data and it says 0%, and you have vibes that say that can’t be true, who should we believe?Do you work with lots of companies and see large AI success stories?Or do you just vibe that you personally find AI useful so it must also be a business success?Look, I honestly don’t care, but I think “it must be false” is also unsubstantiated hyperbole.
sentences.zh: 听着，如果他们有数据并且说 0%，而你有一种感觉说这不可能是真的，我们应该相信谁？你是否与很多公司合作并看到大型人工智能成功故事？或者你只是觉得你个人认为人工智能有用，所以它也一定是商业上的成功？听着，老实说我不在乎，但我认为“它一定是假的”也是未经证实的夸张。

sentences.en: If an agency says they see no AI success, I see no particular reason to believe they’re lying.They’re not saying AI can’t be a success.
sentences.zh: 如果一个机构说他们认为人工智能没有成功，我认为没有特别的理由相信他们在撒谎。他们并不是说人工智能不能成功。

sentences.en: They’re saying they haven’t seen it.
sentences.zh: 他们说他们没见过。

sentences.en: That matches my experience too.
sentences.zh: 这也符合我的经历。

sentences.en: Proven AI success stories seem…
sentences.zh: 经过验证的人工智能成功故事似乎……

sentences.en: vague, when you dig into the details, in my personal experience.It doesn’t seem surprising to me.
sentences.zh: 模糊，当你深入研究细节时，根据我的个人经验。这对我来说并不奇怪。

blocks.html_en:
Their previous article on AI shows a pretty strident ham-handedness. https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48002795

In general I find their submissions tend towards extreme grandiosity. I find I really appreciate people who have some nuance about the world, can see some duality, and the many many many submissions here are (I admit) often quite fun and enjoyable, but spoken much more from a bully pulpit perspective, with a zeal and self certainty that I find rarely coincides with truth-seeking.

sentences.en: Their previous article on AI shows a pretty strident ham-handedness.
sentences.zh: 他们之前关于人工智能的文章显示出相当强硬的笨手笨脚的态度。

sentences.en: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48002795In general I find their submissions tend towards extreme grandiosity.
sentences.zh: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48002795总的来说，我发现他们提交的内容倾向于极端宏大。

sentences.en: I find I really appreciate people who have some nuance about the world, can see some duality, and the many many many submissions here are (I admit) often quite fun and enjoyable, but spoken much more from a bully pulpit perspective, with a zeal and self certainty that I find rarely coincides with truth-seeking.
sentences.zh: 我发现我真的很欣赏那些对世界有一些细微差别的人，可以看到一些二元性，这里的很多很多提交的内容（我承认）通常非常有趣和令人愉快，但更多地是从讲坛的角度讲的，带着一种我发现很少与追求真理相一致的热情和自信。

blocks.html_en: I have, in two wildly different companies, on opposite sides of the globe, witnessed leadership go all in on using LLMs to write external documents that would have significant legal and financial consequences for any inaccurate statements or numbers included. There's always a "we'll have our experts read and fact check everything before we send it out," but I have a strongly held view that people whose expertise is not in editing/fact checking tend to be far worse and sloppier at it than they are at writing things correctly the first time.

sentences.en: I have, in two wildly different companies, on opposite sides of the globe, witnessed leadership go all in on using LLMs to write external documents that would have significant legal and financial consequences for any inaccurate statements or numbers included.
sentences.zh: 我曾在位于地球两端的两家截然不同的公司中目睹过领导层全力以赴地利用法学硕士来撰写外部文件，这些文件中包含的任何不准确的陈述或数字都会产生重大的法律和财务后果。

sentences.en: There's always a "we'll have our experts read and fact check everything before we send it out," but I have a strongly held view that people whose expertise is not in editing/fact checking tend to be far worse and sloppier at it than they are at writing things correctly the first time.
sentences.zh: 总是有这样一句话：“在我们发送出去之前，我们会让我们的专家阅读并核实所有内容”，但我强烈认为，那些不擅长编辑/事实核查的人往往比他们第一次正确编写内容时更糟糕、更草率。

blocks.html_en:
> Checking out a parallel copy of our Go repository and telling the AI to rewrite the whole thing in Zig while I work on something else just so I can keep my job.

> Was it just sales fluff? The answer was a lot more interesting.... Executives at their customers were saying absurd things about achieving 100x productivity, and this meant that if any executive at the vendor said that these gains were not plausible, it would undermine the credibility of the customer’s executive, be perceived as an attack (or heresy), and possibly result in an enterprise contract cancellation.

A lot of excellent anecdotes here.

sentences.en: > Checking out a parallel copy of our Go repository and telling the AI to rewrite the whole thing in Zig while I work on something else just so I can keep my job.> Was it just sales fluff?
sentences.zh: > 检查我们的 Go 存储库的并行副本，并告诉人工智能在 Zig 中重写整个内容，而我则在做其他事情，这样我就可以保住工作。> 这只是销售上的废话吗？

sentences.en: The answer was a lot more interesting....
sentences.zh: 答案更有趣......

sentences.en: Executives at their customers were saying absurd things about achieving 100x productivity, and this meant that if any executive at the vendor said that these gains were not plausible, it would undermine the credibility of the customer’s executive, be perceived as an attack (or heresy), and possibly result in an enterprise contract cancellation.A lot of excellent anecdotes here.
sentences.zh: 客户的高管们对实现 100 倍的生产力提出了荒谬的说法，这意味着如果供应商的任何高管说这些收益不合理，就会损害客户高管的可信度，被视为攻击（或异端邪说），并可能导致企业合同被取消。这里有很多精彩的轶事。

sentences.en: Executives at their customers were saying absurd things about achieving 100x productivity, and this meant that if any executive at the vendor said that these gains were not plausible, it would undermine the credibility of the customer’s executive, be perceived as an attack (or heresy), and possibly result in an enterprise contract cancellation.
sentences.zh: 客户的高管们对实现 100 倍的生产力提出了荒谬的说法，这意味着如果供应商的任何高管表示这些收益不合理，就会损害客户高管的可信度，被视为攻击（或异端邪说），并可能导致企业合同取消。

blocks.html_en: A lot of excellent anecdotes here.

sentences.en: A lot of excellent anecdotes here.
sentences.zh: 这里有很多精彩的轶事。

blocks.html_en:
> All of the AI projects we have observed as a team are failing. Every single one – we have seen 0% success in a year and a half,

What is an "AI project"? The post doesn't define it.

Is it writing some software from scratch? Using an LLM chatbot by non-coders, either internally or externally? Or something else entirely?

Some examples would really help.

sentences.en: > All of the AI projects we have observed as a team are failing.
sentences.zh: > 我们作为一个团队观察到的所有人工智能项目都失败了。

sentences.en: Every single one – we have seen 0% success in a year and a half,What is an "AI project"?
sentences.zh: 每一个——我们在一年半的时间里都看到了 0% 的成功，什么是“AI 项目”？

sentences.en: The post doesn't define it.Is it writing some software from scratch?
sentences.zh: 帖子没有定义它。它是从头开始编写一些软件吗？

sentences.en: Using an LLM chatbot by non-coders, either internally or externally?
sentences.zh: 非编码人员在内部或外部使用 LLM 聊天机器人？

sentences.en: Or something else entirely?Some examples would really help.
sentences.zh: 或者完全是别的什么？一些例子确实会有帮助。

blocks.html_en:
Their company does data projects. That plus context makes me think they’re talking about internal work process automation type of work, although it also seems like they’re talking about conversational interfaces (chatbots).

I completely buy the “emperor’s new clothes” argument for work process automation. I’m surprised they don’t address AI-assisted engineering, which seems to be going positively for a lot of folks (although I have doubts about its sustainability). I disagree about the success of chatbots, if the problem is narrowly-defined and chosen properly. My previous company built a conversational interface to a vector database and saw good results. (Although, arguably, the vector database was the real magic, and a traditional UI would have been faster and more accurate.)

In general, I think OP is more right than wrong, though, particularly about the AI mania and unrealistic expectations sweeping the C-suite.

sentences.en: Their company does data projects.
sentences.zh: 他们的公司做数据项目。

sentences.en: That plus context makes me think they’re talking about internal work process automation type of work, although it also seems like they’re talking about conversational interfaces (chatbots).I completely buy the “emperor’s new clothes” argument for work process automation.
sentences.zh: 再加上上下文让我认为他们正在谈论内部工作流程自动化类型的工作，尽管他们似乎也在谈论会话界面（聊天机器人）。我完全相信工作流程自动化的“皇帝的新衣”论点。

sentences.en: I’m surprised they don’t address AI-assisted engineering, which seems to be going positively for a lot of folks (although I have doubts about its sustainability).
sentences.zh: 我很惊讶他们没有解决人工智能辅助工程问题，这对很多人来说似乎都是积极的（尽管我对其可持续性表示怀疑）。

sentences.en: I disagree about the success of chatbots, if the problem is narrowly-defined and chosen properly.
sentences.zh: 如果问题定义得很窄并且选择得当，我不同意聊天机器人的成功。

sentences.en: My previous company built a conversational interface to a vector database and saw good results.
sentences.zh: 我之前的公司构建了一个矢量数据库的对话界面，并看到了良好的结果。

sentences.en: (Although, arguably, the vector database was the real magic, and a traditional UI would have been faster and more accurate.)In general, I think OP is more right than wrong, though, particularly about the AI mania and unrealistic expectations sweeping the C-suite.
sentences.zh: （尽管可以说，矢量数据库才是真正的魔力，而传统的 UI 会更快、更准确。） 总的来说，我认为 OP 是对多于错，特别是关于人工智能狂热和席卷最高管理层的不切实际的期望。

blocks.html_en:
That Ford story was really misleading. It wasn't about modern LLMs, and the way it was reported implied that Ford had fired and then hired people but if you read closer that wasn't necessarily the case at all - it sounded more like they were re-hiring people who had retired because they needed expertise that had left the company.

You need to get through the Bloomberg paywall: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-25/ford-has-...

> Over the last three years, Ford says it has hired 350 veteran engineers, many of them former employees and others from suppliers, to help address seemingly intractable quality woes that have cost the automaker billions. [...]

> “We had been relying more and more on automated quality systems” and not getting the desired results, Galhotra said. “We brought back technical specialists” and “they hunt for failure points before a part ever reaches the plant floor.”

(I made these points on the HN thread about it 3 weeks ago and got voted down and I'm still salty about it https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48674446#48675045 )

sentences.en: That Ford story was really misleading.
sentences.zh: 福特的故事确实具有误导性。

sentences.en: It wasn't about modern LLMs, and the way it was reported implied that Ford had fired and then hired people but if you read closer that wasn't necessarily the case at all - it sounded more like they were re-hiring people who had retired because they needed expertise that had left the company.You need to get through the Bloomberg paywall: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-25/ford-has-...> Over the last three years, Ford says it has hired 350 veteran engineers, many of them former employees and others from suppliers, to help address seemingly intractable quality woes that have cost the automaker billions. [...]> “We had been relying more and more on automated quality systems” and not getting the desired results, Galhotra said.
sentences.zh: 这与现代法学硕士无关，报道的方式暗示福特先解雇了人，然后又雇用了人员，但如果你仔细阅读，情况根本不一定如此 - 听起来更像是他们重新雇用已经退休的人，因为他们需要离开公司的专业知识。你需要通过彭博社付费墙：https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-25/ford-has-...> 在过去的三年里，福特说该公司聘请了 350 名经验丰富的工程师，其中许多是前员工，其他人来自供应商，以帮助解决看似棘手的质量问题，这些问题已使该汽车制造商损失了数十亿美元。 [...]> “我们越来越依赖自动化质量系统”，但没有得到预期的结果，Galhotra 说。

sentences.en: “We brought back technical specialists” and “they hunt for failure points before a part ever reaches the plant floor.”(I made these points on the HN thread about it 3 weeks ago and got voted down and I'm still salty about it https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48674446#48675045 )
sentences.zh: “我们带回了技术专家”和“他们在零件到达工厂车间之前寻找故障点。”（三周前我在 HN 帖子上提出了这些观点，但被否决了，我仍然对此感到不满 https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48674446#48675045 ）

blocks.html_en: I think it's not clear from the article why they left (e.g. could be anything from retirement to going to work at another firm/contracting to being fired to switching careers), and likely it's going to be a mix, plus not all were previous Ford employees. Similarly the "AI" isn't clearly defined (but like you I would be surprised if it were LLMs). I suspect though why the article exists (and a possible source of your downvotes) is signalling against "AI", which if Ford wants more expert employees (given their issues), is something Ford wants to present.

sentences.en: I think it's not clear from the article why they left (e.g. could be anything from retirement to going to work at another firm/contracting to being fired to switching careers), and likely it's going to be a mix, plus not all were previous Ford employees.
sentences.zh: 我认为文章中并不清楚他们离开的原因（例如，可能是从退休到去另一家公司工作/签约到被解雇到转行等任何原因），而且很可能是混合的，而且并非所有人都是以前的福特员工。

sentences.en: Similarly the "AI" isn't clearly defined (but like you I would be surprised if it were LLMs).
sentences.zh: 同样，“AI”也没有明确定义（但像你一样，如果它是法学硕士，我会感到惊讶）。

sentences.en: I suspect though why the article exists (and a possible source of your downvotes) is signalling against "AI", which if Ford wants more expert employees (given their issues), is something Ford wants to present.
sentences.zh: 我怀疑这篇文章存在的原因（以及你投反对票的可能来源）是在发出反对“人工智能”的信号，如果福特想要更多的专家员工（考虑到他们的问题），这就是福特想要展示的东西。

blocks.html_en:
As @simonw said, the Ford example isn’t a good one.

As for AI-assisted engineering going well, I think the jury is still out. Here on HN and with the engineers I know, you see people claiming multiples of productivity on coding tasks. But you also see people complaining about drowning in slop PRs.

I think there’s a lot of confounding factors to these reports. The type of work matters a lot: bug fixing good, prototyping good, big legacy codebases not so much, but maybe good for increased understanding. The type of automation matters: aggressive autocomplete good, vibe coding bad, dark factory (vibe coding with fancy harnesses and auto-“correcting” eval loops) questionable.

And then finally, the perennial mistake our industry makes, which is to value speed of creation over maintenance costs. Personally, I think this is where AI-assisted engineering is going to fall down really hard, but the jury’s still out on that one.

Anyway, there’s a really big spread in experiences with AI, that I think chalk up more to all this context rather than religion and belief. OP didn’t address it at all, which I think is a big gap in their essay, but I do think think they describe the executive-level mania pretty well.

sentences.en: As @simonw said, the Ford example isn’t a good one.As for AI-assisted engineering going well, I think the jury is still out.
sentences.zh: 正如 @simonw 所说，福特的例子并不是一个好的例子。至于人工智能辅助工程进展顺利，我认为还没有定论。

sentences.en: Here on HN and with the engineers I know, you see people claiming multiples of productivity on coding tasks.
sentences.zh: 在 HN 和我认识的工程师中，你会看到人们声称编码任务的生产力成倍提高。

sentences.en: But you also see people complaining about drowning in slop PRs.I think there’s a lot of confounding factors to these reports.
sentences.zh: 但你也看到人们抱怨淹没在污秽的公关中。我认为这些报告有很多令人困惑的因素。

sentences.en: The type of work matters a lot: bug fixing good, prototyping good, big legacy codebases not so much, but maybe good for increased understanding.
sentences.zh: 工作类型很重要：错误修复很好，原型设计很好，大型遗留代码库不是那么重要，但可能有助于增加理解。

sentences.en: The type of automation matters: aggressive autocomplete good, vibe coding bad, dark factory (vibe coding with fancy harnesses and auto-“correcting” eval loops) questionable.And then finally, the perennial mistake our industry makes, which is to value speed of creation over maintenance costs.
sentences.zh: 自动化的类型很重要：积极的自动完成功能良好，振动编码不好，黑暗工厂（使用花哨的线束和自动“纠正”评估循环进行振动编码）值得怀疑。最后，我们行业常犯的错误，即重视创建速度而不是维护成本。

sentences.en: Personally, I think this is where AI-assisted engineering is going to fall down really hard, but the jury’s still out on that one.Anyway, there’s a really big spread in experiences with AI, that I think chalk up more to all this context rather than religion and belief.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我认为这就是人工智能辅助工程将会严重失败的地方，但目前还没有定论。无论如何，人工智能的经验确实存在很大的差异，我认为这更多地归因于所有这些背景，而不是宗教和信仰。

sentences.en: OP didn’t address it at all, which I think is a big gap in their essay, but I do think think they describe the executive-level mania pretty well.
sentences.zh: OP 根本没有解决这个问题，我认为这是他们文章中的一个很大的空白，但我确实认为他们很好地描述了高管层的狂热。

blocks.html_en:
> As for AI-assisted engineering going well, I think the jury is still out.

Anecdotally, AI-assisted engineering has helped me flesh out ideas or to learn extremely complicated APIs faster than trying to understand the docs (which usually are labyrinthine). MS COM ones, for example. I can go read the docs but it's easier to get a quick idea of what I need to do if I ask Claude to provide me an example of doing something specific with it, because MS's code samples (particularly their full ones in, say, the windows Desktop SDK repo) have always been annoying for me to wade through because I have to filter out a bunch of noise. I can't (and won't) try to guestimate "productivity" improvements though, but as an assistant AI has (somewhat) helped. I still do all the engineering work though. Along with it giving me tips on using more modern language features for languages like C++.

sentences.en: > As for AI-assisted engineering going well, I think the jury is still out.Anecdotally, AI-assisted engineering has helped me flesh out ideas or to learn extremely complicated APIs faster than trying to understand the docs (which usually are labyrinthine).
sentences.zh: > 至于人工智能辅助工程是否进展顺利，我认为还没有定论。有趣的是，人工智能辅助工程帮助我充实了想法，或者比试图理解文档（通常是迷宫般的）更快地学习极其复杂的 API。

sentences.en: MS COM ones, for example.
sentences.zh: 例如，MS COM。

sentences.en: I can go read the docs but it's easier to get a quick idea of what I need to do if I ask Claude to provide me an example of doing something specific with it, because MS's code samples (particularly their full ones in, say, the windows Desktop SDK repo) have always been annoying for me to wade through because I have to filter out a bunch of noise.
sentences.zh: 我可以去阅读文档，但如果我要求 Claude 为我提供一个使用它做特定事情的示例，那么我会更容易快速了解我需要做什么，因为 MS 的代码示例（特别是 Windows Desktop SDK 存储库中的完整代码示例）一直让我费力浏览，因为我必须过滤掉一堆噪音。

sentences.en: I can't (and won't) try to guestimate "productivity" improvements though, but as an assistant AI has (somewhat) helped.
sentences.zh: 虽然我不能（也不会）尝试预测“生产力”的改进，但作为助理人工智能（在某种程度上）有所帮助。

sentences.en: I still do all the engineering work though.
sentences.zh: 不过，我仍然做所有的工程工作。

sentences.en: Along with it giving me tips on using more modern language features for languages like C++.
sentences.zh: 除此之外，它还为我提供了如何在 C++ 等语言中使用更现代的语言功能的技巧。

blocks.html_en:
The Microsoft API docs are a special case where i'd say you pretty much need LLMs nowadays because after a bunch of document format conversions over the years they degraded massively.

If you can find some MSDN CDs/DVDs from the early 2000s, the content is much better (and you can clearly see that the current docs are often missing descriptions and names for method arguments or even entire paragraphs).

blocks.html_en:
> I disagree about the success of chatbots, if the problem is narrowly-defined and chosen properly.

If you can narrow the problem down, then you could design a much better interface for it than a text box and free form text (unless that's the better solution).

As for as AI assisted engineering goes, the thing is that after some time with a project, you already have much of the workflows and routines nailed down as scripts and other various combinations of tooling. And unless it's spaghetti code, you will have various snippets you can copy from for new code. The one thing I've observed about AI projects is that there's often little technical design coherence about them. It's always a kitchen sink of technologies and practices.

sentences.en: > I disagree about the success of chatbots, if the problem is narrowly-defined and chosen properly.If you can narrow the problem down, then you could design a much better interface for it than a text box and free form text (unless that's the better solution).As for as AI assisted engineering goes, the thing is that after some time with a project, you already have much of the workflows and routines nailed down as scripts and other various combinations of tooling.
sentences.zh: > 我不同意聊天机器人的成功，如果问题是狭隘定义和选择正确的。如果你能缩小问题的范围，那么你可以为它设计一个比文本框和自由格式文本更好的界面（除非那是更好的解决方案）。至于人工智能辅助工程，问题是，经过一段时间的项目后，你已经将大部分工作流程和例程确定为脚本和其他各种工具组合。

sentences.en: And unless it's spaghetti code, you will have various snippets you can copy from for new code.
sentences.zh: 除非它是意大利面条代码，否则您将拥有可以复制的各种片段以获取新代码。

sentences.en: The one thing I've observed about AI projects is that there's often little technical design coherence about them.
sentences.zh: 我观察到的关于人工智能项目的一件事是，它们通常缺乏技术设计的一致性。

sentences.en: It's always a kitchen sink of technologies and practices.
sentences.zh: 它始终是技术和实践的厨房水槽。

blocks.html_en:
> If you can narrow the problem down, then you could design a much better interface for it than a text box and free form text (unless that's the better solution).

Yes, I agree, in that the chatbot we built probably would have worked just as well with a traditional UI, and would have been done a lot faster. But it would have been a lot less sexy (actually important for the bottom line!) and there are future directions that could take advantage of the conversational interface that’s potentially better than a traditional UI.

On the down side, good chatbots are really frikkin difficult to write. These things (LLMs) are not reliable at scale. The basic functionality came together in weeks. Getting it to behave consistently and obey guardrails took months, and even then we had to accept a low level of failed conversations.

sentences.en: > If you can narrow the problem down, then you could design a much better interface for it than a text box and free form text (unless that's the better solution).Yes, I agree, in that the chatbot we built probably would have worked just as well with a traditional UI, and would have been done a lot faster.
sentences.zh: > 如果你能缩小问题范围，那么你可以为它设计一个比文本框和自由格式文本更好的界面（除非那是更好的解决方案）。是的，我同意，因为我们构建的聊天机器人可能与传统 UI 一样工作，而且速度会快得多。

sentences.en: But it would have been a lot less sexy (actually important for the bottom line!)
sentences.zh: 但它会少很多性感（实际上对于底线很重要！）

sentences.en: and there are future directions that could take advantage of the conversational interface that’s potentially better than a traditional UI.On the down side, good chatbots are really frikkin difficult to write.
sentences.zh: 未来的方向可以利用比传统 UI 更好的对话界面。 不利的一面是，好的聊天机器人真的很难编写。

sentences.en: These things (LLMs) are not reliable at scale.
sentences.zh: 这些东西（法学硕士）在规模上并不可靠。

sentences.en: The basic functionality came together in weeks.
sentences.zh: 基本功能在几周内就完成了。

sentences.en: Getting it to behave consistently and obey guardrails took months, and even then we had to accept a low level of failed conversations.
sentences.zh: 让它表现一致并遵守护栏需要几个月的时间，即便如此，我们也不得不接受低水平的失败对话。

> But it would have been a lot less sexy (actually important for the bottom line!

That’s what the author have been saying. They do for nice demos which sell the illusion of having Jarvis in text format, but the usefulness is not really proven. And that may be important business wise. But as far as end users is concerned, there’s not a lot of productivity boost.

blocks.html_en:
They run a consulting company called "Hermit Tech". Their websites has an olde style font.

They boast about "ancient techniques" from books written prior to the year 2000

> For non-executive management who might be struggling to deliver things that feel beyond their control, we have ancient techniques (see: books written between 1986 and 1999) to turn your team into the envy of the organisation, and we can drop in directly to get your team the resources it needs to save a struggling project.

So yeah, of course these people hate AI and everything about it.

No serious company is reaching out to these people for help with their AI project.

sentences.en: They run a consulting company called "Hermit Tech".
sentences.zh: 他们经营着一家名为“Hermit Tech”的咨询公司。

sentences.en: Their websites has an olde style font.They boast about "ancient techniques" from books written prior to the year 2000> For non-executive management who might be struggling to deliver things that feel beyond their control, we have ancient techniques (see: books written between 1986 and 1999) to turn your team into the envy of the organisation, and we can drop in directly to get your team the resources it needs to save a struggling project.So yeah, of course these people hate AI and everything about it.No serious company is reaching out to these people for help with their AI project.
sentences.zh: 他们的网站采用老式字体。他们吹嘘 2000 年之前编写的书籍中的“古老技术”> 对于那些可能正在努力交付感觉超出其控制范围的事情的非执行管理人员，我们拥有古老的技术（请参阅：1986 年至 1999 年间编写的书籍），可以让您的团队成为组织羡慕的对象，并且我们可以直接为您的团队提供挽救苦苦挣扎的项目所需的资源。所以，是的，这些人当然讨厌人工智能和有关的一切没有一家认真的公司会向这些人寻求人工智能项目的帮助。

blocks.html_en:
That has ever been the case. As soon as it works reliably, it's not "AI" any more. Take spellcheckers or collaborative filtering as examples, but there are lots more. Hofstadter in G.E.B. said it well:

> There is a related “Theorem” about progress in AI: once some mental function is programmed, people soon cease to consider it as an essential ingredient of “real thinking”. The ineluctable core of intelligence is always in that next thing which hasn’t yet been programmed. This “Theorem” was first proposed to me by Larry Tesler, so I call it Tesler’s Theorem: “AI is whatever hasn’t been done yet.”

sentences.en: That has ever been the case.
sentences.zh: 情况从来都是如此。

sentences.en: As soon as it works reliably, it's not "AI" any more.
sentences.zh: 一旦它可靠地工作，它就不再是“人工智能”了。

sentences.en: Take spellcheckers or collaborative filtering as examples, but there are lots more.
sentences.zh: 以拼写检查器或协作过滤为例，但还有更多。

sentences.en: Hofstadter in G.E.B.
sentences.zh: 霍夫施塔特在 G.E.B.

sentences.en: said it well:> There is a related “Theorem” about progress in AI: once some mental function is programmed, people soon cease to consider it as an essential ingredient of “real thinking”.
sentences.zh: 说得好：> 关于人工智能的进步，有一个相关的“定理”：一旦某种心理功能被编程，人们很快就会不再将其视为“真实思维”的重要组成部分。

sentences.en: The ineluctable core of intelligence is always in that next thing which hasn’t yet been programmed.
sentences.zh: 智力不可避免的核心总是存在于尚未被编程的下一个事物中。

sentences.en: This “Theorem” was first proposed to me by Larry Tesler, so I call it Tesler’s Theorem: “AI is whatever hasn’t been done yet.”
sentences.zh: 这个“定理”是拉里·特斯勒（Larry Tesler）首先向我提出的，所以我称之为特斯勒定理：“人工智能是尚未完成的事情。”

blocks.html_en:
In this moment, the opposite is happening. Everything is getting called "AI", whether it uses LLMs to prompt LLMs about how to prompt LLMs, uses "conventional" machine learning, or just looks mysterious enough that they can expect the market to not ask questions.

I am reminded of "game AI", which for the most part has historically been just giant decision trees, encoded one way or another, because if you hook up any sort of real AI to a game entity or collection of game entities that does any sort of learning or training, even simple 1980s-era reinforcement learning, it turns out the game entities will roflstomp the human players, and the human players aren't interested in paying for that experience. We've been calling those collections of if statements and for loops "AI" for a long time, though, because who wants to hear about how deliberately stupid their opponents are?

sentences.en: In this moment, the opposite is happening.
sentences.zh: 此时此刻，相反的情况正在发生。

sentences.en: Everything is getting called "AI", whether it uses LLMs to prompt LLMs about how to prompt LLMs, uses "conventional" machine learning, or just looks mysterious enough that they can expect the market to not ask questions.I am reminded of "game AI", which for the most part has historically been just giant decision trees, encoded one way or another, because if you hook up any sort of real AI to a game entity or collection of game entities that does any sort of learning or training, even simple 1980s-era reinforcement learning, it turns out the game entities will roflstomp the human players, and the human players aren't interested in paying for that experience.
sentences.zh: 一切都被称为“人工智能”，无论是使用法学硕士来提示法学硕士如何提示法学硕士，使用“传统”机器学习，或者只是看起来足够神秘，以至于他们可以期望市场不会提出问题。我想起了“游戏人工智能”，它在很大程度上历史上只是巨大的决策树，以某种方式编码，因为如果你将任何类型的真实人工智能连接到一个游戏实体或一组游戏实体，它们会进行任何类型的学习或训练，即使是简单的学习或训练20 世纪 80 年代的强化学习，事实证明游戏实体会碾压人类玩家，而人类玩家对为这种体验付费不感兴趣。

sentences.en: We've been calling those collections of if statements and for loops "AI" for a long time, though, because who wants to hear about how deliberately stupid their opponents are?
sentences.zh: 不过，我们长期以来一直将这些 if 语句和 for 循环的集合称为“AI”，因为谁想听他们的对手是多么故意愚蠢呢？

blocks.html_en: Sure, or maybe the actual applications of “AI” are small and unobtrusive, like the dictation of doctor’s notes example, and it’s not actually the massive revolution it’s claimed to be.

blocks.html_en: Perhaps, but I think what people mean by intelligence is something that learns and adapts. If LLMs couldn’t do in context learning I don’t think people would think of them as AI, more as a kind of queryable database via natural language. There are other algorithms that learn and adapt, but in much more narrow circumstances that most people won’t obviously interact with.

sentences.en: Perhaps, but I think what people mean by intelligence is something that learns and adapts.
sentences.zh: 也许吧，但我认为人们所说的智力是指能够学习和适应的东西。

blocks.html_en: Right, and my point is that now that is the bar. If you'd told me in 2008 that markov chains would be smart enough to one-shot even a trivial video game from scratch I would certainly have called that AI even if you had to do some major rigamarole to get it to work.

sentences.en: Right, and my point is that now that is the bar.
sentences.zh: 是的，我的观点是，现在就是酒吧了。

sentences.en: If you'd told me in 2008 that markov chains would be smart enough to one-shot even a trivial video game from scratch I would certainly have called that AI even if you had to do some major rigamarole to get it to work.

sentences.zh: 是的，我的观点是，现在就是酒吧了。

blocks.html_en:
Because AI meant "make computers smart like people".

We taught the computer to spell check, but the computer still didn't feel smart like a person, just smart like a machine so that obviously wasn't AI. We taught it to do algebra, same thing. With LLMs though, now it really does feel like an artificial human, so this time it really is AI.

sentences.en: Because AI meant "make computers smart like people".We taught the computer to spell check, but the computer still didn't feel smart like a person, just smart like a machine so that obviously wasn't AI.
sentences.zh: 因为人工智能的意思是“让计算机像人一样聪明”。我们教计算机进行拼写检查，但计算机仍然感觉不像人那么聪明，只是像机器一样聪明，所以这显然不是人工智能。

sentences.en: We taught it to do algebra, same thing.
sentences.zh: 我们教它做代数，同样的事情。

sentences.en: With LLMs though, now it really does feel like an artificial human, so this time it really is AI.
sentences.zh: 不过，有了法学硕士，现在它确实感觉像一个人造人，所以这次它真的是人工智能。

blocks.html_en:
This post feels like its correct but also doesn't align with my own personal usage of claude for writing advanced sql and python code. I haven't with my own eyes seen an AI chatbot actually deliver a user experience that lets them query data with natural language BUT I have personally experienced writing extremely advanced queries using natural language and it is absolutely able to get close to (by my estimate) 80-90% of the way there.

There surely are companies out there using AI in such a way that is actually advancing them above and beyond their competition. They are probably quietly doing it rather than announcing it loudly.

The whole point of the article is about the big corps with hordes of management and people in them. My argument is that they have always been that way. Before AI it was "data science and analytics" or (as the author says) "blockchain".

sentences.en: This post feels like its correct but also doesn't align with my own personal usage of claude for writing advanced sql and python code.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章感觉它是正确的，但也不符合我个人使用 claude 编写高级 sql 和 python 代码的习惯。

sentences.en: I haven't with my own eyes seen an AI chatbot actually deliver a user experience that lets them query data with natural language BUT I have personally experienced writing extremely advanced queries using natural language and it is absolutely able to get close to (by my estimate) 80-90% of the way there.There surely are companies out there using AI in such a way that is actually advancing them above and beyond their competition.
sentences.zh: 我还没有亲眼看到人工智能聊天机器人实际上提供了一种用户体验，让他们可以用自然语言查询数据，但我个人有使用自然语言编写极其高级的查询的经验，并且它绝对能够接近（据我估计）80-90%。肯定有一些公司以这样的方式使用人工智能，实际上使他们超越了竞争对手。

sentences.en: They are probably quietly doing it rather than announcing it loudly.The whole point of the article is about the big corps with hordes of management and people in them.
sentences.zh: 他们可能正在悄悄地做这件事，而不是大声宣布。这篇文章的重点是关于拥有大量管理层和人员的大公司。

sentences.en: My argument is that they have always been that way.
sentences.zh: 我的观点是他们一直都是这样。

sentences.en: Before AI it was "data science and analytics" or (as the author says) "blockchain".
sentences.zh: 在人工智能之前，它是“数据科学和分析”或（如作者所说）“区块链”。

blocks.html_en:
Maybe maybe not. In large organisations for each person creating value with LLMs there are probably more destroying value. Bob used to only waste one person time with LLMs he can waste the entire organisations time.

Anyone can use an LLM make a bad ideas sound like a good idea. I imagine this will lead to insane amounts of productivity loss as the entire organisation ends up pivoting to follow the bad idea of a mediocre VP etc.

sentences.en: Maybe maybe not.
sentences.zh: 也许也许不是。

sentences.en: In large organisations for each person creating value with LLMs there are probably more destroying value.
sentences.zh: 在大型组织中，对于每个通过法学硕士创造价值的人来说，可能存在更多的破坏性价值。

sentences.en: Bob used to only waste one person time with LLMs he can waste the entire organisations time.Anyone can use an LLM make a bad ideas sound like a good idea.
sentences.zh: 鲍勃过去只在法学硕士上浪费了一个人的时间，但他却浪费了整个组织的时间。任何人都可以利用法学硕士让一个坏主意听起来像一个好主意。

sentences.en: I imagine this will lead to insane amounts of productivity loss as the entire organisation ends up pivoting to follow the bad idea of a mediocre VP etc.
sentences.zh: 我想这将导致生产力大幅下降，因为整个组织最终会转向追随平庸副总裁等的坏主意。

blocks.html_en:
Don't agree with the hyperbole in the article but I think the broad strokes are there. I'm the same as you, AI has excelled at small tasks and daily chores. Like writing a complex query in seconds that used to take an hour to work out.

Doing feature development seems to take just as long or productivity gains are modest (where I work at least) and I think the reason is touched on in the article. Most orgs are just terminally bad at building software.

sentences.en: Don't agree with the hyperbole in the article but I think the broad strokes are there.
sentences.zh: 不同意文章中的夸张说法，但我认为大体思路就在那里。

sentences.en: I'm the same as you, AI has excelled at small tasks and daily chores.
sentences.zh: 我和你一样，人工智能擅长处理小任务和日常琐事。

sentences.en: Like writing a complex query in seconds that used to take an hour to work out.Doing feature development seems to take just as long or productivity gains are modest (where I work at least) and I think the reason is touched on in the article.
sentences.zh: 就像在几秒钟内编写一个复杂的查询，而过去需要一个小时才能完成。进行功能开发似乎需要很长的时间，或者生产力的提高是适度的（至少在我工作的地方），我认为文章中谈到了原因。

sentences.en: Most orgs are just terminally bad at building software.
sentences.zh: 大多数组织在构建软件方面都极其糟糕。

blocks.html_en: Just this past week, I have had to answer two surveys, from different sources, asking how I use AI in my work. Both had a "choose all that apply" checkbox, and, funnily enough, neither allowed me to select zero options. "This question is mandatory". :)

sentences.en: Just this past week, I have had to answer two surveys, from different sources, asking how I use AI in my work.
sentences.zh: 就在上周，我不得不回答两项来自不同来源的调查，询问我如何在工作中使用人工智能。

sentences.en: Both had a "choose all that apply" checkbox, and, funnily enough, neither allowed me to select zero options.
sentences.zh: 两者都有一个“选择所有适用的”复选框，而且有趣的是，两者都不允许我选择零个选项。

sentences.en: "This question is mandatory". :)
sentences.zh: “这个问题是强制性的”。 :)

> Are companies actually seeing massive productivity gains from their AI adoption? Does any of this sordid affair make sense?

It makes perfect sense for the shovel sellers (nvidia, anthropic)

blocks.html_en:
You are basically calling out the fact that the Emperor has no clothes. Many said this before. While it is a true statement, it is not going to help. Because, as you rightly said, it is a mania - like the tulip mania of 17th century or the manias of many forms today. The mania continues to evolve and flourish through it's peak and then go down. For that matter, there is hardly anything that is not a mania. Think of agile processes, timesheets, LoC based productivity, ...

The corporate mindset keeps going through different mania at different times. It could be initiated by some consulting gurus (processes), or some security nerds (strap yourself down until you can't move), or peer pressure (fear of missing out), or presentation goals (show that you are a AI-powered and modern company).

We can't remove or stop manias. Infact that is not the goal. The music should go on and the dance should go on. Everyone is in this dance - customers, businesses, supply chains, governments, thinkers and philosophers. It's a world-wide dance. So it's OK. The music track won't last forever. It will change and dance will change.

sentences.en: You are basically calling out the fact that the Emperor has no clothes.
sentences.zh: 你基本上是在喊出皇帝没有穿衣服的事实。

sentences.en: Many said this before.
sentences.zh: 以前很多人都这么说过。

sentences.en: While it is a true statement, it is not going to help.
sentences.zh: 虽然这是一个真实的陈述，但它不会有任何帮助。

sentences.en: Because, as you rightly said, it is a mania - like the tulip mania of 17th century or the manias of many forms today.
sentences.zh: 因为，正如您所说，这是一种狂热——就像 17 世纪的郁金香狂热或当今多种形式的狂热一样。

sentences.en: The mania continues to evolve and flourish through it's peak and then go down.
sentences.zh: 这种狂热持续发展并蓬勃发展，直至达到顶峰，然后逐渐下降。

sentences.en: For that matter, there is hardly anything that is not a mania.
sentences.zh: 就此而言，几乎没有什么不是狂热。

sentences.en: Think of agile processes, timesheets, LoC based productivity, ...The corporate mindset keeps going through different mania at different times.
sentences.zh: 想想敏捷流程、时间表、基于 LoC 的生产力……企业思维在不同时间不断经历不同的狂热。

sentences.en: It could be initiated by some consulting gurus (processes), or some security nerds (strap yourself down until you can't move), or peer pressure (fear of missing out), or presentation goals (show that you are a AI-powered and modern company).We can't remove or stop manias.
sentences.zh: 它可能是由一些咨询专家（流程）或一些安全呆子（把自己绑起来直到你无法移动），或同侪压力（害怕错过）或演示目标（表明你是一家人工智能驱动的现代公司）发起的。我们无法消除或阻止狂热。

sentences.en: Infact that is not the goal.
sentences.zh: 事实上这不是目标。

sentences.en: The music should go on and the dance should go on.
sentences.zh: 音乐应该继续，舞蹈也应该继续。

sentences.en: Everyone is in this dance - customers, businesses, supply chains, governments, thinkers and philosophers.
sentences.zh: 每个人都在跳舞——客户、企业、供应链、政府、思想家和哲学家。

sentences.en: It's a world-wide dance.
sentences.zh: 这是一种世界性的舞蹈。

sentences.en: So it's OK.
sentences.zh: 所以没关系。

sentences.en: The music track won't last forever.
sentences.zh: 音乐曲目不会永远持续下去。

sentences.en: It will change and dance will change.
sentences.zh: 它会改变，舞蹈也会改变。

blocks.html_en: This mania can also be called "herd behavior" or "crowd psychology". There was that book a while ago about the intelligence of crowds, but far more common is the stupidity and insanity of crowds. Business and politics are mainly driven by it, but the tech industry routinely falls to hype, trends, and decades-long mistakes that seem obvious in hindsight.

sentences.en: This mania can also be called "herd behavior" or "crowd psychology".
sentences.zh: 这种狂热也可以称为“从众行为”或“从众心理”。

sentences.en: There was that book a while ago about the intelligence of crowds, but far more common is the stupidity and insanity of crowds.
sentences.zh: 不久前有一本关于群体智慧的书，但更常见的是群体的愚蠢和疯狂。

sentences.en: Business and politics are mainly driven by it, but the tech industry routinely falls to hype, trends, and decades-long mistakes that seem obvious in hindsight.
sentences.zh: 商业和政治主要受其驱动，但科技行业经常陷入炒作、趋势和长达数十年的错误，事后看来，这些错误似乎是显而易见的。

blocks.html_en: You all are missing the larger context. Not everyone here is equal. There are certain people who control economic organizations, set their goals and priorities, generate hype and fluff to increase sales, push everyone to be more productive, dont do the actual programming work. And then there is everyone else. This is how global capitalism is structured.

sentences.en: You all are missing the larger context.
sentences.zh: 你们都忽略了更大的背景。

sentences.en: Not everyone here is equal.
sentences.zh: 这里并不是每个人都是平等的。

sentences.en: There are certain people who control economic organizations, set their goals and priorities, generate hype and fluff to increase sales, push everyone to be more productive, dont do the actual programming work.
sentences.zh: 有些人控制着经济组织，设定他们的目标和优先事项，制造炒作和虚假信息以增加销售额，推动每个人提高生产力，但不做实际的编程工作。

sentences.en: And then there is everyone else.
sentences.zh: 然后还有其他人。

sentences.en: This is how global capitalism is structured.
sentences.zh: 这就是全球资本主义的结构。

blocks.html_en: For what it’s worth „Extraordinary Popular Delusions and the Madness of Crowds“, while entertaining, isn’t considered a reliable source as it bases most of its story telling on satirical descriptions and reporting of past events and doesn’t do a good job of digging into the actual historical context and dynamics

sentences.en: For what it’s worth „Extraordinary Popular Delusions and the Madness of Crowds“, while entertaining, isn’t considered a reliable source as it bases most of its story telling on satirical descriptions and reporting of past events and doesn’t do a good job of digging into the actual historical context and dynamics
sentences.zh: 就其价值而言，《非凡的流行错觉和人群的疯狂》虽然很有趣，但并不被认为是可靠的来源，因为它的大部分故事讲述都是基于讽刺性的描述和对过去事件的报道，并且没有很好地挖掘实际的历史背景和动态

blocks.html_en: It's super hard to time the market. I would say the sanest approach is to have cash on hand, choose AI adjacent companies with solid fundamentals and buy their shares once the bubble pops. It's very hard to make money being an outright bear, but somewhat easier if you're an early phase/post recovery bull.

sentences.en: It's super hard to time the market.
sentences.zh: 把握市场时机非常困难。

sentences.en: I would say the sanest approach is to have cash on hand, choose AI adjacent companies with solid fundamentals and buy their shares once the bubble pops.
sentences.zh: 我想说，最明智的做法是手头有现金，选择基本面扎实的人工智能相关公司，并在泡沫破灭时购买其股票。

sentences.en: It's very hard to make money being an outright bear, but somewhat easier if you're an early phase/post recovery bull.
sentences.zh: 作为一个彻头彻尾的熊市很难赚钱，但如果你是一个早期/复苏后的牛市，赚钱就容易一些。

sentences.zh: 我想说，最明智的做法是手头有现金，选择基本面扎实的人工智能相关公司，并在泡沫破灭时购买其股票。

sentences.en: It's very hard to make money being an outright bear, but somewhat easier if you're an early phase/post recovery bull.
sentences.zh: 作为一个彻头彻尾的熊市很难赚钱，但如果你是一个早期/复苏后的牛市，赚钱就容易一些。

blocks.html_en:
Everyone knows the ivory towers are full of people who shouldnt be there. Its marvelous to see them crumble even if you arent in the chain gang below.

Nothing will improve until things get bad enough. You need enough greedy yes men doing quailty control on airplanes to escalate.

It took me decades to understand the use of and need for escalation in big organisations.

I didnt understand seemingly unproductive strict job descriptions either. Hilarious situations with 10 people doing nothing at all their entire shift (really nothing) while i had work todo on my own that really required 5 people. A few days later someone showed up to tell me the qualty was below average. LOL

Now i know i should do only half a shift worth of work. When they come complaint about it i say: very good, write it down, make the official report.

Then i hear nothing and a year later its two people with work for 5 scheduled. I tell them to slow down but we still do 2.5 shifts because they dont understand how escalation works.

The nummers now show we are 5 times as productive which isnt good for the company. The beurocracy is slow to adapt and all it has is numbers.

For many years i tried to do all of the work but that means nothing is wrong. The numbers say all is fine most of the time. Someone grinning at how much work i did isnt going to get recorded or processed.

If it looks like an unattended LLM can do a better job it means you dont know what you are talking about. If you fire everyone who noticed you might buy time but reality will catch up.

It reminds me of when they first put computers in trains in NL, they ran on windows 3.11 and no one trusted it to do anything. The solution was to give it all the data so that it could display a nice overview but it didnt control anything. Lots of trains drove around with a blue screen of death or a boot error. If there was a problem it was slightly harder to diagnose but it wouldnt drive if [say] a door was open. If the gui said a door was open you could just ignore it. On its own it means someone has to replace a sensor. If it also didnt move anymore the message is a real issue.

I imagine LLMs are wonderful for that kind of thing.

sentences.en: Everyone knows the ivory towers are full of people who shouldnt be there.
sentences.zh: 大家都知道象牙塔里挤满了不该呆在那里的人。

sentences.en: Its marvelous to see them crumble even if you arent in the chain gang below.Nothing will improve until things get bad enough.
sentences.zh: 即使你不在下面的铁链团伙中，看到它们崩溃也是很棒的。除非事情变得足够糟糕，否则一切都不会改善。

sentences.en: You need enough greedy yes men doing quailty control on airplanes to escalate.It took me decades to understand the use of and need for escalation in big organisations.I didnt understand seemingly unproductive strict job descriptions either.
sentences.zh: 你需要足够多贪婪的唯唯诺诺的人在飞机上进行质量控制来升级。我花了几十年的时间才理解大型组织中升级的使用和需要。我也不理解看似毫无成效的严格工作描述。

sentences.en: Hilarious situations with 10 people doing nothing at all their entire shift (really nothing) while i had work todo on my own that really required 5 people.
sentences.zh: 搞笑的情况是，10 个人整个轮班什么都不做（真的什么都不做），而我自己有工作要做，确实需要 5 个人。

sentences.en: A few days later someone showed up to tell me the qualty was below average.
sentences.zh: 几天后，有人出现告诉我质量低于平均水平。

sentences.en: LOLNow i know i should do only half a shift worth of work.
sentences.zh: 哈哈现在我知道我应该只做一半的轮班工作。

sentences.en: When they come complaint about it i say: very good, write it down, make the official report.Then i hear nothing and a year later its two people with work for 5 scheduled.
sentences.zh: 当他们抱怨时，我说：很好，写下来，做正式报告。然后我什么也没听到，一年后，两个人的工作安排了 5 个。

sentences.en: I tell them to slow down but we still do 2.5 shifts because they dont understand how escalation works.The nummers now show we are 5 times as productive which isnt good for the company.
sentences.zh: 我告诉他们放慢速度，但我们仍然实行 2.5 个轮班，因为他们不明白升级是如何运作的。现在的数字显示我们的生产力提高了 5 倍，这对公司不利。

sentences.en: The beurocracy is slow to adapt and all it has is numbers.For many years i tried to do all of the work but that means nothing is wrong.
sentences.zh: 官僚机构适应起来很慢，它所拥有的只是数字。多年来我试图做所有的工作，但这意味着没有任何问题。

sentences.en: The numbers say all is fine most of the time.
sentences.zh: 数字表明大多数时候一切都很好。

sentences.en: Someone grinning at how much work i did isnt going to get recorded or processed.If it looks like an unattended LLM can do a better job it means you dont know what you are talking about.
sentences.zh: 有人嘲笑我做了多少工作不会被记录或处理。如果看起来无人值守的法学硕士可以做得更好，那就意味着你不知道自己在说什么。

sentences.en: If you fire everyone who noticed you might buy time but reality will catch up.It reminds me of when they first put computers in trains in NL, they ran on windows 3.11 and no one trusted it to do anything.
sentences.zh: 如果你解雇所有注意到你的人，你可能会赢得时间，但现实会赶上。这让我想起当他们第一次在荷兰的火车上安装计算机时，他们运行的是 Windows 3.11，没有人相信它能做任何事情。

sentences.en: The solution was to give it all the data so that it could display a nice overview but it didnt control anything.
sentences.zh: 解决方案是给它所有的数据，以便它可以显示一个很好的概述，但它不能控制任何东西。

sentences.en: Lots of trains drove around with a blue screen of death or a boot error.
sentences.zh: 许多火车在行驶时都会出现蓝屏死机或启动错误。

sentences.en: If there was a problem it was slightly harder to diagnose but it wouldnt drive if [say] a door was open.
sentences.zh: 如果出现问题，诊断起来会稍微困难一些，但如果（比如说）门打开，它就不会行驶。

sentences.en: If the gui said a door was open you could just ignore it.
sentences.zh: 如果图形用户界面显示门已打开，您可以忽略它。

sentences.en: On its own it means someone has to replace a sensor.
sentences.zh: 就其本身而言，这意味着必须有人更换传感器。

sentences.en: If it also didnt move anymore the message is a real issue.I imagine LLMs are wonderful for that kind of thing.
sentences.zh: 如果它也不再移动，那么该消息就是一个真正的问题。我想法学硕士非常适合这类事情。

blocks.html_en: The AI mania has been really fascinating to witness because on the surface it’s surprising that so many otherwise intelligent people have fallen into it. But I suppose intelligence as a concept is multifaceted and doesn’t include wisdom. I also wonder if it has to do with personality, where the people whose personality best suits leadership roles are more susceptible to this psychosis. I think there is also an archetype of “nerd” who believes they are smarter than they are and has all sorts of surface beliefs about AI from sci-fi that makes them susceptible.

sentences.en: The AI mania has been really fascinating to witness because on the surface it’s surprising that so many otherwise intelligent people have fallen into it.
sentences.zh: 见证人工智能的狂热确实令人着迷，因为从表面上看，令人惊讶的是，有这么多原本聪明的人陷入其中。

sentences.en: But I suppose intelligence as a concept is multifaceted and doesn’t include wisdom.
sentences.zh: 但我认为智力作为一个概念是多方面的，并且不包括智慧。

sentences.en: I also wonder if it has to do with personality, where the people whose personality best suits leadership roles are more susceptible to this psychosis.
sentences.zh: 我还想知道这是否与性格有关，性格最适合领导角色的人更容易患上这种精神病。

sentences.en: I think there is also an archetype of “nerd” who believes they are smarter than they are and has all sorts of surface beliefs about AI from sci-fi that makes them susceptible.
sentences.zh: 我认为还有一种“书呆子”的原型，他们认为自己比实际更聪明，并且对科幻小说中的人工智能有各种表面的信念，这使他们很容易受到影响。

blocks.html_en: The AI mania has been really fascinating to witness because on the surface it’s surprising that so many otherwise intelligent people have fallen into it. But I suppose intelligence as a concept is multifaceted and doesn’t include wisdom. I also wonder if it has to do with personality, where the people whose personality best suits leadership roles are more susceptible to this psychosis. I think there is also an archetype of “nerd” who believes they are smarter than they are and has all sorts of surface beliefs about AI from sci-fi that makes them susceptible.

blocks.html_en: It’s also a bit of a dilemma; if your boss has AI mania, and you buying into it or not is the difference between being promoted, keeping your job, passed up or even fired, the rational course of action for self-preservation is to also buy into the mania.

sentences.en: It’s also a bit of a dilemma; if your boss has AI mania, and you buying into it or not is the difference between being promoted, keeping your job, passed up or even fired, the rational course of action for self-preservation is to also buy into the mania.
sentences.zh: 这也是一个两难的问题；如果你的老板有人工智能狂热，而你是否接受它，就决定了升职、保住工作、被解雇甚至被解雇，那么自我保护的理性行动方针就是也接受这种狂热。

blocks.html_en: You can't get a fad like this with a true believer rate of 0. Too unstable. You have to have some true believers in the mix to see this situation arise, preferably widely distributed throughout the population.

blocks.html_en:
Something I think about quite often with HN is there are probably people on this forum that have friends / family who work at AI companies or are highly monetarily invested in AI themselves.

I don't think everyone on this forum is as objective as we'd like to think. I know I'm not. A large part of why I dislike AI is because I view it as making my life, personally, worse. It has completely fucked up the career I've been building for going on two decades

And all I hear is "adapt or die" from assholes who are chugging the AI koolaid by the bucketfull

sentences.en: I know I'm not.
sentences.zh: 我知道我不是。

blocks.html_en:
It's the bell curve meme every time - very, very often stupid people end up being "wiser" than moderately intelligent people.

Meanwhile the really intelligent people end up with beliefs surprisingly close directionally to the stupid people who've barely thought about an issue, just fleshed out with 100x the detail.

A little knowledge is a dangerous thing

sentences.en: It's the bell curve meme every time - very, very often stupid people end up being "wiser" than moderately intelligent people.Meanwhile the really intelligent people end up with beliefs surprisingly close directionally to the stupid people who've barely thought about an issue, just fleshed out with 100x the detail.A little knowledge is a dangerous thing
sentences.zh: 每次都是钟形曲线模因 - 很多时候，愚蠢的人最终会比中等聪明的人“更聪明”。与此同时，真正聪明的人最终的信念与那些几乎没有思考过问题、只是用 100 倍细节充实的愚蠢人的信念惊人地接近。一知半解是一件危险的事情

blocks.html_en:
I've seen both variants of this where the midwit is enthusiastic about the impending singularity and where the midwit is saying it's all a bubble.

Everyone's just portraying a strawman of the other side with new formats.

sentences.en: I've seen both variants of this where the midwit is enthusiastic about the impending singularity and where the midwit is saying it's all a bubble.Everyone's just portraying a strawman of the other side with new formats.
sentences.zh: 我见过这两种情况，其中助产士对即将到来的奇点充满热情，而助产士则说这一切都是泡沫。每个人都只是用新的格式描绘另一边的稻草人。

blocks.html_en:
Yes true, I can see that actually.

I feel though that if you picked a random sample of members of the public and asked them about the probability of the singularity you would get blank looks from most people on the left tail even if you explained it.

Whereas if you had the statement "ChatGPT can be useful for some things, but it isn't going to fundamentally change the world" you would get a lot of people on both tails agreeing and the people who disagreed would be clustered in the middle.

Maybe that's just my bias speaking though and actually I'm the midwit

sentences.en: Yes true, I can see that actually.I feel though that if you picked a random sample of members of the public and asked them about the probability of the singularity you would get blank looks from most people on the left tail even if you explained it.Whereas if you had the statement "ChatGPT can be useful for some things, but it isn't going to fundamentally change the world" you would get a lot of people on both tails agreeing and the people who disagreed would be clustered in the middle.Maybe that's just my bias speaking though and actually I'm the midwit
sentences.zh: 是的，确实如此，我实际上可以看到这一点。我觉得，如果你随机抽取公众成员并询问他们奇点的概率，即使你解释了这一点，你也会得到左尾大多数人的茫然表情。而如果你有这样的说法“ChatGPT 对某些事情有用，但它不会从根本上改变世界”，你会得到很多人在两条尾巴上同意，而不同意的人会聚集在中间。也许这只是虽然我有偏见，但实际上我是助产士

blocks.html_en: It's a very interesting technology, so it leaves some shockwaves while it's impacting the world. Over time this will be absorbed and the insanity will die out, but it's quite transformative, just like cars and the internet were (to name a few).

sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，这些都会被吸收，疯狂也会消失，但它具有相当大的变革性，就像汽车和互联网一样（仅举几例）。

blocks.html_en: It's a very interesting technology, so it leaves some shockwaves while it's impacting the world. Over time this will be absorbed and the insanity will die out, but it's quite transformative, just like cars and the internet were (to name a few).

blocks.html_en:
The other day I asked Claude Haiku (the dumbest model) if dark energy was just the self-energy of the Higgs field. I know barely anything about quantum physics. I just wanted some example of AI writing style - something like "You're absolutely right! That's a key insight into a load-bearing ..."

Instead it spat back a bunch of physics related stuff, alleged problems with the idea ("there are 30 orders of magnitude difference between that and dark energy, and why the Higgs field and not any of the other fields, and did you know about axions which are somehow relevant, and ..."). I humored it by saying "well the Higgs field is the only one with a nonzero vacuum expectation value" and it told me I'm absolutely right, that's a key insight, etc etc etc. Then I asked it to tell me more about axions and it said some stuff about CP violation, instantons, but then it made some connections to dark energy and to the Higgs field and said I was clever to bring that up.

Now from my point of view I had to deliberately keep in mind that I was talking to a Markov chain and that I was myself acting as a Markov chain, and it's likely that none of these statements actually meant anything in physics. I can very easily see how someone who didn't realize that would be captured and then write a blog post proclaiming themselves to have made a discovery in physics.

sentences.en: The other day I asked Claude Haiku (the dumbest model) if dark energy was just the self-energy of the Higgs field.
sentences.zh: 有一天，我问克劳德·俳句（最愚蠢的模型）暗能量是否只是希格斯场的自能。

sentences.en: I know barely anything about quantum physics.
sentences.zh: 我对量子物理学几乎一无所知。

sentences.en: I just wanted some example of AI writing style - something like "You're absolutely right!
sentences.zh: 我只是想要一些人工智能写作风格的例子 - 比如“你完全正确！

sentences.en: That's a key insight into a load-bearing ..."Instead it spat back a bunch of physics related stuff, alleged problems with the idea ("there are 30 orders of magnitude difference between that and dark energy, and why the Higgs field and not any of the other fields, and did you know about axions which are somehow relevant, and ...").
sentences.zh: 这是对承重的一个关键洞察……”相反，它吐出了一堆与物理相关的东西，声称这个想法存在问题（“它和暗能量之间有 30 个数量级的差异，以及为什么是希格斯场而不是任何其他场，你是否知道轴子在某种程度上相关，以及……”）。

sentences.en: I humored it by saying "well the Higgs field is the only one with a nonzero vacuum expectation value" and it told me I'm absolutely right, that's a key insight, etc etc etc. Then I asked it to tell me more about axions and it said some stuff about CP violation, instantons, but then it made some connections to dark energy and to the Higgs field and said I was clever to bring that up.Now from my point of view I had to deliberately keep in mind that I was talking to a Markov chain and that I was myself acting as a Markov chain, and it's likely that none of these statements actually meant anything in physics.
sentences.zh: 我幽默地说“嗯，希格斯场是唯一一个具有非零真空期望值的场”，它告诉我我是绝对正确的，这是一个关键的见解，等等等等。然后我要求它告诉我更多关于轴子的信息，它说了一些关于CP破坏、瞬间子的东西，但随后它与暗能量和希格斯场建立了一些联系，并说我很聪明地提出了这一点。现在从我的角度来看，我必须刻意记住我正在和一个马尔可夫链，我自己就是马尔可夫链，很可能这些陈述实际上在物理学中都没有任何意义。

sentences.en: I can very easily see how someone who didn't realize that would be captured and then write a blog post proclaiming themselves to have made a discovery in physics.
sentences.zh: 我可以很容易地看到那些没有意识到这一点的人会被捕获，然后写一篇博客文章宣称自己在物理学方面取得了发现。

blocks.html_en:
I think AI is only part of the problem. The Multi-crisis ahead, makes it even less of a proposition to be in charge right now- and AI offers a "responsibility" cope out for already stressed to the limit human systems who have no solutions for the problems, because there often arent any.

Take climate change- you have torrential rainfalls, sweping away whole city-parts in mountanous regions, in some enormous russian roulett. And it doesnt even factor into building evaluations because then it would basically reduce the prevalent pension scheme to cinders.

You have dry months in europe now, where some thrown cigarett butt could ignite a firestorm- and the obvious solution is to remove the dangerous greenery from the burbs. Nobody does it though.

And that is just the plain sight visible layer of this shit cake. If i was some missguided fool into heroics and leadership and signed up for a little more then i could take and fake- i would long for some magic box that lowers the burden too. Those up there are human after all.

sentences.en: I think AI is only part of the problem.
sentences.zh: 我认为人工智能只是问题的一部分。

sentences.en: The Multi-crisis ahead, makes it even less of a proposition to be in charge right now- and AI offers a "responsibility" cope out for already stressed to the limit human systems who have no solutions for the problems, because there often arent any.Take climate change- you have torrential rainfalls, sweping away whole city-parts in mountanous regions, in some enormous russian roulett.
sentences.zh: 未来的多重危机，使得现在掌控一切不再是一个建议——而人工智能为已经承受压力的人类系统提供了一种“责任”，这些系统没有解决问题的办法，因为通常没有任何解决方案。以气候变化为例——暴雨席卷了山区的整个城市部分，在一些巨大的俄罗斯轮盘中。

sentences.en: And it doesnt even factor into building evaluations because then it would basically reduce the prevalent pension scheme to cinders.You have dry months in europe now, where some thrown cigarett butt could ignite a firestorm- and the obvious solution is to remove the dangerous greenery from the burbs.
sentences.zh: 它甚至没有被纳入建筑评估中，因为那样的话，它基本上会将普遍的养老金计划化为灰烬。欧洲现在正经历干旱的几个月，一些扔掉的烟头可能会引发一场大火，而显而易见的解决方案就是清除郊区的危险绿化。

sentences.en: Nobody does it though.And that is just the plain sight visible layer of this shit cake.
sentences.zh: 但没有人这样做。而这只是这个狗屎蛋糕的明显可见层。

sentences.en: If i was some missguided fool into heroics and leadership and signed up for a little more then i could take and fake- i would long for some magic box that lowers the burden too.
sentences.zh: 如果我是一个被误导的英雄英雄和领导力的傻瓜，并且报名参加了更多的比赛，那么我就可以接受和假装——我会渴望一些能减轻负担的神奇盒子。

sentences.en: Those up there are human after all.
sentences.zh: 上面的人毕竟是人类。

blocks.html_en: We have villages near cities, whos native populations get displaced due to the price hikes and then we have "settlements" which is roughly equivalent to suburbs for those who are more well todo - while the less wealthy are going into the old buildings the even less wealthy natives have vacated.

sentences.en: We have villages near cities, whos native populations get displaced due to the price hikes and then we have "settlements" which is roughly equivalent to suburbs for those who are more well todo - while the less wealthy are going into the old buildings the even less wealthy natives have vacated.
sentences.zh: 我们在城市附近有村庄，当地居民因物价上涨而流离失所，然后我们有“定居点”，对于那些富裕的人来说，这大致相当于郊区——而不太富裕的人则搬进了旧建筑，而更不富裕的当地人则搬出了旧楼。

blocks.html_en:
The range of ai approaches ranges from inappropriately betting on it to wholly ignoring it both of which are dumb.

It helps to think of AI as analogous to previous game changers like electricity, computers, internet. If your business is bad or you have no game plan beyond "use internet" your are fucked. But at the same time almost every business has some angle in which these technologies are valuable and necessary and ignoring them is also death.

blocks.html_en:
As someone who uses LLMS for coding, ideas validation and research, I think the article is biased against AI forctge wrong reasons.

If you know what you are looking for and know what “a solution looks like”, AI is amazing at distilling ideas. If you have no clue, the AI will return “clueless” solutions.

It is just like before the AI: there are people who know how to search the web, read and understand documentation and so on. And then there are peole who are incapable.

AI is naking the latter category fail incredibly fast. Really, nothing new under the sun: garbage in, garbage out.

sentences.en: As someone who uses LLMS for coding, ideas validation and research, I think the article is biased against AI forctge wrong reasons.If you know what you are looking for and know what “a solution looks like”, AI is amazing at distilling ideas.
sentences.zh: 作为使用 LLMS 进行编码、想法验证和研究的人，我认为这篇文章对 AI 的偏见是错误的。如果你知道自己在寻找什么，并且知道“解决方案是什么样子”，那么 AI 在提炼想法方面非常出色。

sentences.en: If you have no clue, the AI will return “clueless” solutions.It is just like before the AI: there are people who know how to search the web, read and understand documentation and so on.
sentences.zh: 如果你没有任何线索，人工智能将返回“无知”的解决方案。就像人工智能出现之前一样：有人知道如何搜索网络、阅读和理解文档等等。

sentences.en: And then there are peole who are incapable.AI is naking the latter category fail incredibly fast.
sentences.zh: 还有一些人是无能的。人工智能正在使后一类人以惊人的速度失败。

sentences.en: Really, nothing new under the sun: garbage in, garbage out.
sentences.zh: 确实，阳光下没有什么新鲜事：垃圾进来，垃圾出去。

blocks.html_en:
My hunch is that AI tools are probabilistically good enough to deceive most casual users in the beginning, so beliefs are built on these foundations. LLMs handle simple tasks reasonably well so their perceived 'intelligence' is easily convincing for many users (often managers and executives).

However, this illusion immediately falls apart during actual stress-testing like writing code for a complex, battle-tested production environments. Non-technical folks can effortlessly spin up simple web apps or tweak a frontend so now they conclude that software engineers are becoming redundant.

And even if there are obvious examples of all of this backfiring no one believes it...

sentences.en: My hunch is that AI tools are probabilistically good enough to deceive most casual users in the beginning, so beliefs are built on these foundations.
sentences.zh: 我的预感是，人工智能工具在概率上足以在一开始就欺骗大多数临时用户，因此信念是建立在这些基础之上的。

sentences.en: LLMs handle simple tasks reasonably well so their perceived 'intelligence' is easily convincing for many users (often managers and executives).However, this illusion immediately falls apart during actual stress-testing like writing code for a complex, battle-tested production environments.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士可以很好地处理简单的任务，因此他们所感知的“智能”很容易让许多用户（通常是经理和高管）信服。然而，在实际压力测试（例如为复杂的、经过实战考验的生产环境编写代码）过程中，这种幻想立即破灭。

sentences.en: Non-technical folks can effortlessly spin up simple web apps or tweak a frontend so now they conclude that software engineers are becoming redundant.And even if there are obvious examples of all of this backfiring no one believes it...
sentences.zh: 非技术人员可以毫不费力地启动简单的网络应用程序或调整前端，所以现在他们得出结论，软件工程师正在变得多余。即使有所有这些事与愿违的明显例子，也没有人相信......

blocks.html_en: There are a lot of simple web apps out there that haven't been built because of the cost and many more that have been built but cost a lot to maintain. AI seems to work on those and fill in a pretty large space.

sentences.en: There are a lot of simple web apps out there that haven't been built because of the cost and many more that have been built but cost a lot to maintain.
sentences.zh: 有很多简单的 Web 应用程序由于成本原因而尚未构建，还有许多已经构建但维护成本很高的应用程序。

sentences.en: AI seems to work on those and fill in a pretty large space.
sentences.zh: 人工智能似乎可以解决这些问题并填补相当大的空间。
