blocks.html_en:
Probably true. But isn't this a failure of software engineering? We should have plenty of simple ways to build simple apps by now.

Maybe you should be able to go down to your local web app shop, give a couple simple requirements, and get back an app in a week. So why can't you? Well there's actually a good reason for that - the customer would never be satisfied by the result, and building a satisfactory result requires many iterations back and forth. Rapid feedback is essential to software development.

Okay, an alternative is to give the customer an app builder so they can make their own app. We've done this many times in history, it always takes the customer time to make the app, the results are always mediocre, but it does work, why do we keep doing this and then stopping it? I'm not really sure. Why isn't there a visual basic today (apart from Roblox) or even a Frontpage (apart from Squarespace)? My best guess is the customer tires of working on the app or never had time for it in the first place. Why won't AI suffer the same fate? Really no idea.

sentences.en: Probably true.
sentences.zh: 也许是真的。

sentences.en: But isn't this a failure of software engineering?
sentences.zh: 但这难道不是软件工程的失败吗？

sentences.en: We should have plenty of simple ways to build simple apps by now.Maybe you should be able to go down to your local web app shop, give a couple simple requirements, and get back an app in a week.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，我们应该有很多简单的方法来构建简单的应用程序。也许您应该能够前往本地的网络应用程序商店，提出一些简单的要求，然后在一周内获得应用程序。

sentences.en: So why can't you?
sentences.zh: 那你为什么不能呢？

sentences.en: Well there's actually a good reason for that - the customer would never be satisfied by the result, and building a satisfactory result requires many iterations back and forth.
sentences.zh: 嗯，这实际上是有充分理由的 - 客户永远不会对结果感到满意，而构建令人满意的结果需要来回多次迭代。

sentences.en: Rapid feedback is essential to software development.Okay, an alternative is to give the customer an app builder so they can make their own app.
sentences.zh: 快速反馈对于软件开发至关重要。好吧，另一种选择是为客户提供应用程序构建器，以便他们可以制作自己的应用程序。

sentences.en: We've done this many times in history, it always takes the customer time to make the app, the results are always mediocre, but it does work, why do we keep doing this and then stopping it?
sentences.zh: 我们在历史上已经做过很多次了，总是需要客户时间来制作应用程序，结果总是平庸，但它确实有效，为什么我们一直这样做然后停止呢？

sentences.en: I'm not really sure.
sentences.zh: 我不太确定。

sentences.en: Why isn't there a visual basic today (apart from Roblox) or even a Frontpage (apart from Squarespace)?
sentences.zh: 为什么今天没有 Visual Basic（除了 Roblox）甚至没有 Frontpage（除了 Squarespace）？

sentences.en: My best guess is the customer tires of working on the app or never had time for it in the first place.
sentences.zh: 我最好的猜测是，客户厌倦了使用该应用程序，或者一开始就没有时间使用它。

sentences.en: Why won't AI suffer the same fate?
sentences.zh: 为什么人工智能不会遭受同样的命运？

sentences.en: Really no idea.
sentences.zh: 真的不知道。

sentences.en: Rapid feedback is essential to software development.Okay, an alternative is to give the customer an app builder so they can make their own app.
sentences.zh: 快速反馈对于软件开发至关重要。好吧，另一种选择是为客户提供应用程序构建器，以便他们可以制作自己的应用程序。

sentences.en: We've done this many times in history, it always takes the customer time to make the app, the results are always mediocre, but it does work, why do we keep doing this and then stopping it?
sentences.zh: 我们在历史上已经做过很多次了，总是需要客户时间来制作应用程序，结果总是平庸，但它确实有效，为什么我们一直这样做然后停止呢？

sentences.en: I'm not really sure.
sentences.zh: 我不太确定。

sentences.en: Why isn't there a visual basic today (apart from Roblox) or even a Frontpage (apart from Squarespace)?
sentences.zh: 为什么今天没有 Visual Basic（除了 Roblox）甚至没有 Frontpage（除了 Squarespace）？

sentences.en: My best guess is the customer tires of working on the app or never had time for it in the first place.
sentences.zh: 我最好的猜测是，客户厌倦了使用该应用程序，或者一开始就没有时间使用它。

sentences.en: Why won't AI suffer the same fate?
sentences.zh: 为什么人工智能不会遭受同样的命运？

sentences.en: Really no idea.
sentences.zh: 真的不知道。

blocks.html_en: Probably true. But isn't this a failure of software engineering? We should have plenty of simple ways to build simple apps by now.

sentences.en: Probably true.
sentences.zh: 也许是真的。

sentences.en: But isn't this a failure of software engineering?
sentences.zh: 但这难道不是软件工程的失败吗？

sentences.en: We should have plenty of simple ways to build simple apps by now.
sentences.zh: 现在我们应该有很多简单的方法来构建简单的应用程序。

blocks.html_en: Maybe you should be able to go down to your local web app shop, give a couple simple requirements, and get back an app in a week. So why can't you? Well there's actually a good reason for that - the customer would never be satisfied by the result, and building a satisfactory result requires many iterations back and forth. Rapid feedback is essential to software development.

sentences.en: Maybe you should be able to go down to your local web app shop, give a couple simple requirements, and get back an app in a week.
sentences.zh: 也许您应该能够前往当地的网络应用程序商店，提出几个简单的要求，然后在一周内取回应用程序。

sentences.en: So why can't you?
sentences.zh: 那你为什么不能呢？

blocks.html_en:
I could not disagree more. You must have used the better models by now. Even if you only lightly use them for a question from time to time, it must have become clear that these things are pretty damn good and aren't going away?

When doing hard things I need to be 100% in the loop, but I don't have to do anything menial anymore. Imagine being in some CIA control room with 10 emplyees, and you're directing the team. You're still on top of things, just not bogged down with bulky minutiae.

sentences.en: I could not disagree more.
sentences.zh: 我完全不同意。

sentences.en: You must have used the better models by now.
sentences.zh: 您现在一定已经使用过更好的模型了。

sentences.en: Even if you only lightly use them for a question from time to time, it must have become clear that these things are pretty damn good and aren't going away?When doing hard things I need to be 100% in the loop, but I don't have to do anything menial anymore.
sentences.zh: 即使你只是偶尔轻微地使用它们来回答问题，你也一定已经清楚这些东西非常好而且不会消失？当做困难的事情时，我需要 100% 参与循环，但我不必再做任何卑微的事情了。

sentences.en: Imagine being in some CIA control room with 10 emplyees, and you're directing the team.
sentences.zh: 想象一下，在中央情报局的某个控制室里，有 10 名员工，而您正在指挥团队。

sentences.en: You're still on top of things, just not bogged down with bulky minutiae.
sentences.zh: 你仍然掌控一切，只是没有陷入繁琐的细节之中。

blocks.html_en: I love this author's article about saving half a million dollars with a click from a while back. Nikhil, if you're reading this, I have a decent war story about a similar situation (I was lucky enough that there were two such things, so I saved a full million a year and was still denied a $15k raise) and so I was really entertained at your post from a few years back. There are actually a lot of lessons to be learned about corporate politics there, about how you can save someone a million a year in perpetuity and promise to do that again next year (which I could have!) and see them still refuse to pay you a single extra cent.

sentences.en: I love this author's article about saving half a million dollars with a click from a while back.
sentences.zh: 我喜欢这位作者不久前写的关于通过点击节省 50 万美元的文章。

sentences.en: Nikhil, if you're reading this, I have a decent war story about a similar situation (I was lucky enough that there were two such things, so I saved a full million a year and was still denied a $15k raise) and so I was really entertained at your post from a few years back.
sentences.zh: Nikhil，如果你正在读这篇文章，我有一个关于类似情况的不错的战争故事（我很幸运，有两起这样的事情，所以我每年节省了整整一百万美元，但仍然被拒绝加薪 15,000 美元），所以几年前我对你的职位感到非常高兴。

sentences.en: and see them still refuse to pay you a single extra cent.
sentences.zh: 并看到他们仍然拒绝支付你一分钱的额外费用。

blocks.html_en:
The author doesn't distinguish whether these projects failed because of technology and execution, or failed because product market-fit. They simply blame AI being involved.

AI will only help if you use rapid iteration to cheaply/quickly produce ideas. All the normal project failure modes still exist.. Blaming AI because AI is dumb.

I only read up to point 3 because the hyperbole and frothing fervour was overwhelming.

sentences.en: The author doesn't distinguish whether these projects failed because of technology and execution, or failed because product market-fit.
sentences.zh: 作者没有区分这些项目是因为技术和执行而失败，还是因为产品市场契合而失败。

sentences.en: They simply blame AI being involved.AI will only help if you use rapid iteration to cheaply/quickly produce ideas.
sentences.zh: 他们只是指责人工智能的参与。只有当你使用快速迭代来廉价/快速地产生想法时，人工智能才会有所帮助。

sentences.zh: 所有正常的项目失败模式仍然存在。

sentences.en: Blaming AI because AI is dumb.I only read up to point 3 because the hyperbole and frothing fervour was overwhelming.
sentences.zh: 指责人工智能是因为人工智能很愚蠢。我只读到了第 3 点，因为其中的夸张和泡沫热情令人难以承受。

blocks.html_en:
I want to frame my comment here carefully to talk about the narratives, specifically, and accept that we need to stipulate on face value that this is true (it certainly rings true from people I know in real life):

This specific narrative reads like every narrative explanation of the Republican party in the period from the second half of the first Trump administration until now.

Again, I am talking only about the narratives here. But the pervasive narrative around Trump is that privately everyone hates his guts, comes away with a worse impression when they work with him closely, dreads him opening his mouth, thinks his decisions are insane, and they are telling journalists that, at every opportunity, and have done since the 2018 midterms. And yet in public we have committee hearings where any number of irrational topics become a sort of terror zone here rational people have to assert irrational beliefs or pretzel logic to be able to continue; where ordinary absurdities have become cult-like witness-bearing that everyone polices everyone else on. It's like the Hunger Games of litmus tests.

For example the desire for career advancement is a common thread in both of these narratives, even though this is being promoted further up into the kafkaesque absurdity. Both of these things remind me of other things, like somehow everyone in US politics seemingly believes in God and somehow nobody in an international football (soccer) team is gay. Even though these are not plausible.

sentences.en: I want to frame my comment here carefully to talk about the narratives, specifically, and accept that we need to stipulate on face value that this is true (it certainly rings true from people I know in real life):This specific narrative reads like every narrative explanation of the Republican party in the period from the second half of the first Trump administration until now.Again, I am talking only about the narratives here.
sentences.zh: 我想在这里仔细地阐述我的评论，具体地谈论叙述，并接受我们需要从表面上规定这是真的（这对我在现实生活中认识的人来说肯定是正确的）：这个特定的叙述读起来就像共和党在第一届特朗普政府后半段直到现在的每一个叙述解释。再说一次，我在这里只谈论叙述。

sentences.en: But the pervasive narrative around Trump is that privately everyone hates his guts, comes away with a worse impression when they work with him closely, dreads him opening his mouth, thinks his decisions are insane, and they are telling journalists that, at every opportunity, and have done since the 2018 midterms.
sentences.zh: 但围绕特朗普的普遍说法是，私下里每个人都讨厌他的胆量，当他们与他密切合作时，会给他留下更糟糕的印象，害怕他张嘴，认为他的决定很疯狂，他们利用一切机会告诉记者，自 2018 年中期选举以来一直这样做。

sentences.en: And yet in public we have committee hearings where any number of irrational topics become a sort of terror zone here rational people have to assert irrational beliefs or pretzel logic to be able to continue; where ordinary absurdities have become cult-like witness-bearing that everyone polices everyone else on.
sentences.zh: 然而，在公开场合，我们举行委员会听证会，任何数量的非理性话题都成为一种恐怖地带，理性的人必须坚持非理性的信念或椒盐卷饼逻辑才能继续下去；普通的荒唐事变成了邪教式的见证，每个人都在监督其他人。

sentences.en: It's like the Hunger Games of litmus tests.For example the desire for career advancement is a common thread in both of these narratives, even though this is being promoted further up into the kafkaesque absurdity.
sentences.zh: 这就像试金石的饥饿游戏。例如，对职业发展的渴望是这两种叙述中的共同点，尽管这正在进一步提升到卡夫卡式的荒谬。

sentences.en: Both of these things remind me of other things, like somehow everyone in US politics seemingly believes in God and somehow nobody in an international football (soccer) team is gay.
sentences.zh: 这两件事让我想起了其他事情，比如美国政界的每个人似乎都相信上帝，而国际足球队中却没有人是同性恋。

sentences.en: Even though these are not plausible.
sentences.zh: 尽管这些都不太合理。

blocks.html_en:
I want to frame my comment here carefully to talk about the narratives, specifically, and accept that we need to stipulate on face value that this is true (it certainly rings true from people I know in real life):

This specific narrative reads like every narrative explanation of the Republican party in the period from the second half of the first Trump administration until now.

Again, I am talking only about the narratives here. But the pervasive narrative around Trump is that privately everyone hates his guts, comes away with a worse impression when they work with him closely, dreads him opening his mouth, thinks his decisions are insane, and they are telling journalists that, at every opportunity, and have done since the 2018 midterms. And yet in public we have committee hearings where any number of irrational topics become a sort of terror zone here rational people have to assert irrational beliefs or pretzel logic to be able to continue; where ordinary absurdities have become cult-like witness-bearing that everyone polices everyone else on. It's like the Hunger Games of litmus tests.

For example the desire for career advancement is a common thread in both of these narratives, even though this is being promoted further up into the kafkaesque absurdity. Both of these things remind me of other things, like somehow everyone in US politics seemingly believes in God and somehow nobody in an international football (soccer) team is gay. Even though these are not plausible.

sentences.en: But the pervasive narrative around Trump is that privately everyone hates his guts, comes away with a worse impression when they work with him closely, dreads him opening his mouth, thinks his decisions are insane, and they are telling journalists that, at every opportunity, and have done since the 2018 midterms.
sentences.zh: 但围绕特朗普的普遍说法是，私下里每个人都讨厌他的胆量，当他们与他密切合作时，会给他留下更糟糕的印象，害怕他张嘴，认为他的决定很疯狂，他们利用一切机会告诉记者，自 2018 年中期选举以来一直这样做。

sentences.en: And yet in public we have committee hearings where any number of irrational topics become a sort of terror zone here rational people have to assert irrational beliefs or pretzel logic to be able to continue; where ordinary absurdities have become cult-like witness-bearing that everyone polices everyone else on.
sentences.zh: 然而，在公开场合，我们举行委员会听证会，任何数量的非理性话题都成为一种恐怖地带，理性的人必须坚持非理性的信念或椒盐卷饼逻辑才能继续下去；普通的荒唐事变成了邪教式的见证，每个人都在监督其他人。

blocks.html_en:
This is excellent.

> Employees don’t use internal chatbots because companies tend to have low-quality documentation and an LLM is not psychic – it can only know things that have been written down and made accessible.

Then later:

> In one extreme case, I have seen an executive confess that they had never even used ChatGPT or any AI tool in their life, immediately after producing a technical strategy for an organisation with $2B+ in revenue which was entirely centered around AI.

And:

> In fact, we have been forced to opt out of every sale where the lead has expressed anything beyond the most fleeting curiosity in the use of AI in their business. I don’t mean that we’ve heard that they’re interested in AI and elected to drop the contract on moral grounds. I mean that, over the course of the engagement, these people have exhibited a pattern of behavior that has made it near-impossible to sell to them without incurring reputational and legal risk, and are furthermore crafting management environments that I can only describe as cultish, ineffective, and “please dear God, do not let it be on earth as it is on LinkedIn”.

And:

> with one client, we uncovered that staff were totally unaware they had been given licenses for AI tooling, which cast into doubt all productivity claims.

sentences.en: This is excellent.> Employees don’t use internal chatbots because companies tend to have low-quality documentation and an LLM is not psychic – it can only know things that have been written down and made accessible.Then later:> In one extreme case, I have seen an executive confess that they had never even used ChatGPT or any AI tool in their life, immediately after producing a technical strategy for an organisation with $2B+ in revenue which was entirely centered around AI.And:> In fact, we have been forced to opt out of every sale where the lead has expressed anything beyond the most fleeting curiosity in the use of AI in their business.
sentences.zh: 这太棒了。> 员工不使用内部聊天机器人，因为公司往往拥有低质量的文档，而法学硕士不是通灵的 - 它只能知道已经写下来并可以访问的东西。然后：> 在一个极端的例子中，我看到一位高管承认，他们一生中从未使用过 ChatGPT 或任何人工智能工具，在为一家收入超过 20 亿美元、完全以人工智能为中心的组织制定技术战略后立即承认。并且：> 事实上，我们被迫选择在每一次销售中，领导者都表达了对在业务中使用人工智能的最短暂的好奇心。

sentences.en: I don’t mean that we’ve heard that they’re interested in AI and elected to drop the contract on moral grounds.
sentences.zh: 我并不是说我们听说他们对人工智能感兴趣并出于道德原因选择放弃合同。

sentences.en: I mean that, over the course of the engagement, these people have exhibited a pattern of behavior that has made it near-impossible to sell to them without incurring reputational and legal risk, and are furthermore crafting management environments that I can only describe as cultish, ineffective, and “please dear God, do not let it be on earth as it is on LinkedIn”.And:> with one client, we uncovered that staff were totally unaware they had been given licenses for AI tooling, which cast into doubt all productivity claims.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，在参与过程中，这些人表现出了一种行为模式，几乎不可能在不承担声誉和法律风险的情况下向他们出售产品，而且他们还营造了一种我只能形容为邪教、低效和“请亲爱的上帝，不要让它像 LinkedIn 上那样出现在地球上”的管理环境。并且：> 在与一位客户合作时，我们发现员工完全不知道他们已获得人工智能工具的许可证，这令人怀疑所有生产力主张。

blocks.html_en:
It should be possible to exploit a rich person's poor decision making to make them poorer and myself richer. But I don't see how. Why not?

It sounds like the writer of this article could benefit from starting another brand which is the same product but with AI. That brand would make all the delusional AI chatbot sales and absorb the reputational damage when the chatbot destroys companies.

sentences.en: It should be possible to exploit a rich person's poor decision making to make them poorer and myself richer.
sentences.zh: 应该有可能利用富人的错误决策，让他们变得更穷，让我自己变得更富有。

sentences.en: But I don't see how.
sentences.zh: 但我不明白怎么办。

sentences.en: Why not?It sounds like the writer of this article could benefit from starting another brand which is the same product but with AI.
sentences.zh: 为什么不呢？听起来这篇文章的作者可以从创建另一个使用人工智能的相同产品的品牌中受益。

sentences.en: That brand would make all the delusional AI chatbot sales and absorb the reputational damage when the chatbot destroys companies.

blocks.html_en:
AI may have made a few mistakes, e.g. suggesting the US abduct a sitting president (Claude), and maybe went too far with the whole diarrhea-vomit machine thing, but you can't say it doesn't write a clean python script. Maybe if we can just get AI to view humanity as a software problem... and give Palantir the chance it's been waiting for.

One thing is certain; it's here to stay. Now we just need to centralize it into the hands of a small, highly organized group of wise and motivated people, and give it the control it needs to follow through on things, without human oversight and accountability getting in the way.

We should focus on the positive side.*

*The positive side being the classified private versions where the models aren't hindered by guardrails, ethics, moralizing, etc and function as genuine force multipliers.

sentences.en: AI may have made a few mistakes, e.g. suggesting the US abduct a sitting president (Claude), and maybe went too far with the whole diarrhea-vomit machine thing, but you can't say it doesn't write a clean python script.
sentences.zh: 人工智能可能犯了一些错误，例如建议美国绑架一位现任总统（克劳德），也许在整个腹泻呕吐机的事情上走得太远了，但你不能说它没有写一个干净的Python脚本。

sentences.en: Maybe if we can just get AI to view humanity as a software problem...
sentences.zh: 也许如果我们能让人工智能将人类视为一个软件问题......

sentences.zh: 并给 Palantir 一个它一直在等待的机会。有一件事是肯定的；它会留在这里。

sentences.en: Now we just need to centralize it into the hands of a small, highly organized group of wise and motivated people, and give it the control it needs to follow through on things, without human oversight and accountability getting in the way.We should focus on the positive side.**The positive side being the classified private versions where the models aren't hindered by guardrails, ethics, moralizing, etc and function as genuine force multipliers.
sentences.zh: 现在我们只需要把它集中到一小群高度组织化的、明智的、积极进取的人手中，并给予它完成事情所需的控制权，而无需人为监督和问责制的阻碍。我们应该关注积极的一面。**积极的一面是分类的私人版本，其中模型不受护栏、道德、道德说教等的阻碍，并发挥真正的力量倍增器的作用。

blocks.html_en:
PS: the diarrhea machine was a reference to the Active Denial style tech they used to make Maduro's soldiers shit themselves.

I'm no fan of narco, but with all the prejudicial executions of boaters and such, I really think Claude overstepped with the Venezuela plot. You just don't want too many historically unprecedented events happening all at once. The tyranny should be spaced. As I said, a little more sanding down of the harness, AI & Palantir should be ready to permanently solve our problems,

blocks.html_en:
Broadly I don't think this is quite so true, quite such a mortal threat.

What I see are that there are a lot of extremely fake humans, who want and need cover. Who have absurd ridiculous (and often dastardly or sinister) plans. Who want to do things, a-priori. But could never get away with their actions, in any just clear reasoned normal rules of society.

And AI is this new circuit breaker. It's innovative permission to move ridiculously fast and break everything, right now. Take the perhaps old IBM slide and flip it upside down,

> A computer can never be held accountable.

> Therefore a computer must never make a management decision

https://simonwillison.net/2025/Feb/3/a-computer-can-never-be...

The people "using" AI today to "make decisions" are using it because AI cannot be held accountable therefore that is the cover for their decisions.

This is is all such a resounding PKD nightmare, a reality bring invaded by Fake Humans. It was that was already, just gobs of nonsense, the worst liars spreading the most ridiculous memetic caltrap everywhere: Bullshit Asymmetry Principle weaponized against reason to ever higher degrees, Fox News terrormongering advanced and advanced, Hastert Rule obstructionist politics by wicked pedophile protectors and system ruiners and monsters. AI is a rapid accelerant for burning down reality, for propagating the disreality that the fake humans require for existence. Un-people truly from some other dimension, who've worked and worked to get away with their twisted anti- reality over us all.

AI can and does help with a lot of decision making, in good ways. It's an incredibly tool. It can comb through incredible amounts of data. But it's primary use in "decision making" seems to be in deflecting responsibility, in making hideous choices no human system could reasonably make. In concocting fabulations. Both of management design, and endless fuel nightmare disreality slop video to dislodge any last bits of real reality still clinging on (hello ai faked campaign videos!).

blocks.html_en:
Broadly I don't think this is quite so true, quite such a mortal threat.

What I see are that there are a lot of extremely fake humans, who want and need cover. Who have absurd ridiculous (and often dastardly or sinister) plans. Who want to do things, a-priori. But could never get away with their actions, in any just clear reasoned normal rules of society.

And AI is this new circuit breaker. It's innovative permission to move ridiculously fast and break everything, right now. Take the perhaps old IBM slide and flip it upside down,

> A computer can never be held accountable.

> Therefore a computer must never make a management decision

https://simonwillison.net/2025/Feb/3/a-computer-can-never-be...

The people "using" AI today to "make decisions" are using it because AI cannot be held accountable therefore that is the cover for their decisions.

This is is all such a resounding PKD nightmare, a reality bring invaded by Fake Humans. It was that was already, just gobs of nonsense, the worst liars spreading the most ridiculous memetic caltrap everywhere: Bullshit Asymmetry Principle weaponized against reason to ever higher degrees, Fox News terrormongering advanced and advanced, Hastert Rule obstructionist politics by wicked pedophile protectors and system ruiners and monsters. AI is a rapid accelerant for burning down reality, for propagating the disreality that the fake humans require for existence. Un-people truly from some other dimension, who've worked and worked to get away with their twisted anti- reality over us all.

AI can and does help with a lot of decision making, in good ways. It's an incredibly tool. It can comb through incredible amounts of data. But it's primary use in "decision making" seems to be in deflecting responsibility, in making hideous choices no human system could reasonably make. In concocting fabulations. Both of management design, and endless fuel nightmare disreality slop video to dislodge any last bits of real reality still clinging on (hello ai faked campaign videos!).

The "frothing excitement" here is the frothing excitement to destroy society, to be and bring out the most wicked brutal careless world that can be brought upon us, to raise up the Theil-istic/(Octavia) Butler-ian nightmare neofuedalim. It is to escape accountability, to give cover for sin savagery and sabotage.

(Regarding the article, I do think it's worth tempering ones read of this article by reading the authors previous work on AI. Which to me exposes their baises and in my view makes them so vastly unreliable & overdramatic a narrator as to be near worthless. Their other submissions are less greviously clearly full of it, but also tend towards ridiculous over-grandiosity. https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48002795 )

blocks.html_en:
I read the entire post, and I think this company has no expertise at all. So I'd rather they just used AI writing instead.At least Frontier model AI doesn't make such overblown claims.

They proudly claim that every AI project they've observed over the past year and a half had a 0% success rate, and that they've rejected all AI implementation work. While this is evidence that the market is crazy, at its core, it's a painful confession that they have no engineering expertise to implement and control modern AI architectures like RAG, Agentic Workflow, and context window optimization to meet business requirements. I find it fascinating how they're packaging that. It's basically saying, 'We're behind the times.'

There are already products that have achieved results by using AI as part of their development process, yet lumping all different types of AI usage into a single failure category is not only inaccurate but also misleading.

Same goes for the Snowflake Cortex anecdote. Even a freelancer like me can explain technical limitations and distinguish between what's possible and what's not, especially when clients are eager.

There's no engineering analysis in this entire post about why AI fails. No mention of technical bottlenecks like vector DB retrieval quality degradation or prompt injection failures.

I've also worked on RAG for a specific company. For internal knowledge chatbots, it often fails depending on document collection rates and chunking. But none of that is mentioned.

So I understand that AI projects and related things are bad. But there's no analysis of why.

For example, regarding Snowflake, I'm not sure, but did they discuss accuracy in terms of what query set or what ground truth they were using? You're consultants, aren't you?

Honestly, I don't understand why people are excited about this. I'd rather they just used AI. TIt's not about whether human writing is good or bad. It's that this kind of writing feels like a deception of the reader.

When making overgeneralizations, there's a basic minimum standard required.

Saying that making token usage a KPI makes it hard for employees to report is just an 'obvious' fact that's already appeared in far too many essays. Wake up. You're 'consultants.' Consultants are supposed to provide metrics and directions, but all you're doing is shouting into an echo chamber and asking for agreement.

If a significant portion of corporate AI investments are shoddy, you could at least propose specific metrics like document collection rates or user evaluation scores using the very skills you claim to have. I really don't get it.

Just use AI. I wish the OP had used AI. Let me be realistic.

sentences.en: I read the entire post, and I think this company has no expertise at all.
sentences.zh: 我读了整篇文章，我认为这家公司根本没有专业知识。

sentences.en: So I'd rather they just used AI writing instead.At least Frontier model AI doesn't make such overblown claims.They proudly claim that every AI project they've observed over the past year and a half had a 0% success rate, and that they've rejected all AI implementation work.
sentences.zh: 所以我宁愿他们直接用AI写作来代替。至少Frontier model AI不会做出如此夸张的说法。他们自豪地宣称，他们在过去的一年半中观察到的每个AI项目的成功率都是0％，并且他们拒绝了所有AI实施工作。

sentences.en: While this is evidence that the market is crazy, at its core, it's a painful confession that they have no engineering expertise to implement and control modern AI architectures like RAG, Agentic Workflow, and context window optimization to meet business requirements.
sentences.zh: 虽然这证明市场是疯狂的，但从本质上来说，这是一个痛苦的承认，他们没有工程专业知识来实施和控制 RAG、Agentic Workflow 和上下文窗口优化等现代 AI 架构来满足业务需求。

sentences.en: I find it fascinating how they're packaging that.
sentences.zh: 我发现他们的包装方式很有趣。

sentences.en: It's basically saying, 'We're behind the times.'There are already products that have achieved results by using AI as part of their development process, yet lumping all different types of AI usage into a single failure category is not only inaccurate but also misleading.Same goes for the Snowflake Cortex anecdote.
sentences.zh: 它基本上是在说，“我们落后了时代。”已经有一些产品通过使用人工智能作为其开发过程的一部分而取得了成果，但将所有不同类型的人工智能使用归为一个单一的故障类别不仅不准确，而且具有误导性。雪花皮质的轶事也是如此。

sentences.en: Even a freelancer like me can explain technical limitations and distinguish between what's possible and what's not, especially when clients are eager.There's no engineering analysis in this entire post about why AI fails.
sentences.zh: 即使是像我这样的自由职业者也可以解释技术限制并区分什么是可能的，什么是不可能的，尤其是当客户渴望时。整篇文章中没有关于人工智能失败原因的工程分析。

sentences.en: No mention of technical bottlenecks like vector DB retrieval quality degradation or prompt injection failures.I've also worked on RAG for a specific company.
sentences.zh: 没有提及矢量数据库检索质量下降或提示注入失败等技术瓶颈。我也曾为一家特定公司从事过 RAG 工作。

sentences.en: For internal knowledge chatbots, it often fails depending on document collection rates and chunking.
sentences.zh: 对于内部知识聊天机器人来说，它经常会失败，具体取决于文档收集率和分块。

sentences.en: But none of that is mentioned.So I understand that AI projects and related things are bad.
sentences.zh: 但这些都没有被提及。所以我知道人工智能项目和相关的东西很糟糕。

sentences.en: But there's no analysis of why.For example, regarding Snowflake, I'm not sure, but did they discuss accuracy in terms of what query set or what ground truth they were using?
sentences.zh: 但没有分析原因。例如，关于 Snowflake，我不确定，但他们是否讨论了他们使用的查询集或基本事实的准确性？

sentences.en: You're consultants, aren't you?Honestly, I don't understand why people are excited about this.
sentences.zh: 你们是顾问，不是吗？老实说，我不明白为什么人们对此感到兴奋。

sentences.en: I'd rather they just used AI.
sentences.zh: 我宁愿他们只使用人工智能。

sentences.en: TIt's not about whether human writing is good or bad.
sentences.zh: 这与人类写作的好坏无关。

sentences.en: It's that this kind of writing feels like a deception of the reader.When making overgeneralizations, there's a basic minimum standard required.Saying that making token usage a KPI makes it hard for employees to report is just an 'obvious' fact that's already appeared in far too many essays.
sentences.zh: 这种写法感觉像是在欺骗读者。在进行过度概括时，需要有一个基本的最低标准。说将代币使用情况作为 KPI 让员工难以报告只是一个“明显”的事实，已经出现在太多文章中了。

sentences.en: Wake up.
sentences.zh: 醒来吧。

sentences.en: You're 'consultants.'
sentences.zh: 你们是‘顾问’。

sentences.en: Consultants are supposed to provide metrics and directions, but all you're doing is shouting into an echo chamber and asking for agreement.If a significant portion of corporate AI investments are shoddy, you could at least propose specific metrics like document collection rates or user evaluation scores using the very skills you claim to have.
sentences.zh: 顾问应该提供指标和方向，但你所做的只是对着回音室大喊大叫并寻求同意。如果企业人工智能投资的很大一部分是劣质的，你至少可以使用你声称拥有的技能提出具体的指标，例如文档收集率或用户评估分数。

sentences.en: I really don't get it.Just use AI.
sentences.zh: 我真的不明白，就用AI吧。

sentences.en: I wish the OP had used AI.
sentences.zh: 我希望OP使用了人工智能。

sentences.en: Let me be realistic.
sentences.zh: 让我现实一点。

Consultants are supposed to provide metrics and directions, but all you're doing is shouting into an echo chamber and asking for agreement.

sentences.en: Saying that making token usage a KPI makes it hard for employees to report is just an 'obvious' fact that's already appeared in far too many essays.
sentences.zh: 说将代币使用作为 KPI 会让员工难以报告，这只是一个“明显”的事实，已经出现在太多文章中了。

sentences.en: Wake up.
sentences.zh: 醒来吧。

sentences.en: You're 'consultants.'
sentences.zh: 你们是‘顾问’。

sentences.en: Consultants are supposed to provide metrics and directions, but all you're doing is shouting into an echo chamber and asking for agreement.
sentences.zh: 顾问应该提供指标和方向，但你所做的只是对着回音室大喊并寻求同意。

blocks.html_en: If a significant portion of corporate AI investments are shoddy, you could at least propose specific metrics like document collection rates or user evaluation scores using the very skills you claim to have. I really don't get it.

sentences.en: If a significant portion of corporate AI investments are shoddy, you could at least propose specific metrics like document collection rates or user evaluation scores using the very skills you claim to have.
sentences.zh: 如果企业人工智能投资的很大一部分是劣质的，那么您至少可以使用您声称拥有的技能来提出具体的指标，例如文档收集率或用户评估分数。

sentences.en: I really don't get it.
sentences.zh: 我真的不明白。

blocks.html_en: Just use AI. I wish the OP had used AI. Let me be realistic.

sentences.en: Just use AI.
sentences.zh: 只需使用人工智能即可。

sentences.en: I wish the OP had used AI.
sentences.zh: 我希望OP使用了人工智能。

sentences.en: Let me be realistic.
sentences.zh: 让我现实一点。

blocks.html_en:
Clicking the footnote for the weird "All of the AI projects we have observed as a team are failing" is equal parts enlightening and confusing:

> We have rejected all AI implementation work. It is absolutely a gigantic bubble and we have minimized our exposure to it – every single one of our current contracts would be totally unaffected by OpenAI collapsing, save for perhaps some second-order effects such a recession causing a client to become unable to pay us. And there’s nothing we can do to insulate ourselves from that anyway.

Following the link to their company page goes to Hermit Tech, where the primary advertisements for their services are about helping failing projects and troubled teams.

So this is just one huge selection bias example? Start a consulting company for recovering struggling projects, then make claims like "100% of the projects we've seen are struggling"?

There's so much more in this blog post that feels like they're working hard to ignore anything that disagrees with their bubble. Building an AI data pipeline with evals such that you can swap between AI APIs is standard. It's actually part of doing a decent job because you need to select which model hits the right cost/performance tradeoffs and be in a position to pivot when that math changes. Harboring ideas that OpenAI is going to collapse and bring your projects down with it is the kind of talk you hear out of people who don't understand how AI projects work or that there's an ecosystem to it beyond a single company.

The latest projects I'm working on even include open weight models that can be run on reasonable local hardware as cost and performance benchmarks. Even if all of the AI providers collapsed at the same time and nobody offered any services (not going to happen) these projects can still continue on.

It's a very weird time in technology. You can have one foot in a world where people are adopting technologies and using them intelligently, then you can run into articles like this from people who have built their own little self-selecting bubble that confirms all of their ideas who can't even imagine that successful projects exist right now.

sentences.en: Clicking the footnote for the weird "All of the AI projects we have observed as a team are failing" is equal parts enlightening and confusing:> We have rejected all AI implementation work.
sentences.zh: 点击奇怪的脚注“我们作为一个团队观察到的所有人工智能项目都失败了”既具有启发性又令人困惑：>我们拒绝了所有人工智能实施工作。

sentences.en: It is absolutely a gigantic bubble and we have minimized our exposure to it – every single one of our current contracts would be totally unaffected by OpenAI collapsing, save for perhaps some second-order effects such a recession causing a client to become unable to pay us.
sentences.zh: 这绝对是一个巨大的泡沫，我们已经最大限度地减少了对它的风险——我们当前的每一份合同都完全不会受到 OpenAI 崩溃的影响，除了一些二阶效应，例如经济衰退导致客户无法向我们付款。

sentences.en: And there’s nothing we can do to insulate ourselves from that anyway.Following the link to their company page goes to Hermit Tech, where the primary advertisements for their services are about helping failing projects and troubled teams.So this is just one huge selection bias example?
sentences.zh: 无论如何，我们无法采取任何措施来使自己免受这种情况的影响。点击他们公司页面的链接后，会转到 Hermit Tech，他们的服务的主要广告是关于帮助失败的项目和陷入困境的团队。所以这只是一个巨大的选择偏见的例子？

sentences.en: Start a consulting company for recovering struggling projects, then make claims like "100% of the projects we've seen are struggling"?There's so much more in this blog post that feels like they're working hard to ignore anything that disagrees with their bubble.
sentences.zh: 创办一家咨询公司来恢复陷入困境的项目，然后做出诸如“我们见过的 100% 的项目都陷入困境”之类的声明？这篇博文中有太多内容，让人感觉他们正在努力忽略任何与他们的泡沫不一致的东西。

sentences.en: Building an AI data pipeline with evals such that you can swap between AI APIs is standard.
sentences.zh: 使用评估构建 AI 数据管道，以便您可以在 AI API 之间进行交换是标准做法。

sentences.en: It's actually part of doing a decent job because you need to select which model hits the right cost/performance tradeoffs and be in a position to pivot when that math changes.
sentences.zh: 这实际上是做好工作的一部分，因为您需要选择哪种模型能够实现正确的成本/性能权衡，并能够在数学发生变化时进行调整。

sentences.en: Harboring ideas that OpenAI is going to collapse and bring your projects down with it is the kind of talk you hear out of people who don't understand how AI projects work or that there's an ecosystem to it beyond a single company.The latest projects I'm working on even include open weight models that can be run on reasonable local hardware as cost and performance benchmarks.
sentences.zh: 你会从那些不了解 AI 项目如何运作或不了解 AI 项目除了单个公司之外还有生态系统的人那里听到这样的想法：OpenAI 将会崩溃并让你的项目随之崩溃。我正在从事的最新项目甚至包括开放权重模型，这些模型可以在合理的本地硬件上运行作为成本和性能基准。

sentences.en: Even if all of the AI providers collapsed at the same time and nobody offered any services (not going to happen) these projects can still continue on.It's a very weird time in technology.
sentences.zh: 即使所有人工智能提供商同时崩溃并且没有人提供任何服务（不会发生），这些项目仍然可以继续进行。这是技术领域的一个非常奇怪的时代。

sentences.en: You can have one foot in a world where people are adopting technologies and using them intelligently, then you can run into articles like this from people who have built their own little self-selecting bubble that confirms all of their ideas who can't even imagine that successful projects exist right now.
sentences.zh: 你可以一只脚踏入一个人们正在采用技术并明智地使用它们的世界，然后你可能会遇到这样的文章，这些文章来自那些建立了自己的小自我选择泡沫的人，这些泡沫证实了他们所有的想法，他们甚至无法想象现在存在成功的项目。

content_html_zh: 我们大多数人在某种程度上都相信人工智能将彻底改变我们的社会。 至少对我来说，奇点是有意义的。 结果并非如此，但我们并没有接受我们所犯错误的残酷现实，并花一点时间重新评估，而是决定通过在我们能找到的每一个裂缝和缝隙中使用撬棍和凡士林人工智能，并大声宣告进步，来实现我们向自己承诺的未来。这就像一些戴着任天堂动力手套的 90 年代孩子，他们确信这会让他们成为一名黑客。 这不是奇点。我个人迫不及待地希望这一切结束，并希望每个人都能集体回去等待下一张应该解决我们所有问题的金票。我只是不明白为什么有人会认为我们回到了法学硕士之前的世界。 自从 Opus 4.5 发布以来，您还没有使用过它吗？特别是对于不再从事软件行业的人来说，每周没有 40 个小时坐在那里编写代码，代理 AI 是搜索引擎、公告板或编译器水平上的技术飞跃。您实际上可以打开 Claude Code，说：-“将这些带有这些邮箱的域添加到在我的家庭实验室上运行的 SMTP 服务器，通过 mxroute 转发所有传入和传出”-“端口此将 2017 年无人维护的应用程序移植到现代 Android，然后在模拟器上进行测试，并使用我的真实密钥对其进行签名，以便通过 Google Play 进行分发”——“在 Linux、Windows、macOS 和 Android 上获取我的 GitHub Actions 运行器，确保它支持过去十五年以来的所有可用架构，然后循环检查你的输出，直到它正常工作”，它只是在你洗碗的时候把它吐出来。前几天有人谈到“AI 精神病精神病”，它绝对让我难以忘怀。 那些否认这是一件大事的人都在啦啦地。我们现在在模型智能方面确实可能会碰壁，但它仍然会改变游戏规则。它不会回到法学硕士之前的时代，也不会进入一个生成式人工智能乌托邦，在那里我们可以关闭我们的大脑，模型将在没有明确指导的情况下理解人类存在的每一个细微差别。就像区块链仍然存在一样，它只是没有彻底改变权力下放。 它创建了一个新工具，当您想要区块链所能带来的好处时可以实现该工具，同样的事情也会发生在语言模型上。我认为这整件事对我来说最大的认识是大多数人不想关闭他们的大脑。 我认为我们非常天真地认为某种零思想纯粹休闲的假设生活不仅是可以实现的，而且是令人向往的。而这只是...... 不是。 人们想要受到挑战，他们想要阅读人类的材料，他们想要聆听人类的音乐。 当然，那些蹩脚的 Spotify 冒牌艺术家可能会为 Spotify 赚到一些钱。 但人们是去参加那些不存在的巡演，还是转而听《忏悔录 II》并每晚祈祷麦当娜再次巡演？而且，麦当娜，如果你正在读这篇文章：请继续巡演。 请。正确的？ 这感觉就像那些反对社会福利和全民基本收入之类的人一样，因为“人们就是不想工作”。 现实似乎更像是“人们想要工作，他们最想对工作是什么有发言权。” 有多少从事服务工作的人无法接受高等教育，或者买不起吉他，或者在研究藻类之前意外地照顾家庭，等等。就我个人而言，如果我不必担心钱，我会花大部分时间为其他人编织和钩编东西，或者我会帮助我的邻居建立她想要的商业网站，或者我会教孩子们如何编码。 这些是我想做的事情，但在有全职工作的开销的情况下我不能轻易做，除非我把自己弄得精疲力尽，说实话，我宁愿不做。我认为这种情绪来自于那些从工作/掌握中找不到乐趣的人。 随着LLM支持的开发，激励措施发生了很大的变化，但人们对哪些激励措施没有反应。我怀念代码矿> - “将这些带有这些邮箱的域添加到我的家庭实验室上运行的 SMTP 服务器，通过 mxroute 转发所有传入和传出”我读到这里笑了。 你真的尝试过吗？ 我最近试图让 Claude (Opus) 给我一个使用 Postfix 进行简单 SMTP 中继的设置。 我必须不断地努力不包含完全不必要的组件和配置，例如 Dovecot，或者只是忽略我的要求。 即使是克劳德也只是一个半能干的教程答题者。对于那些自己从未做过这样的事情的人来说，结果可能看起来很棒。 他们可能不会像我那样在细节上与克劳德争论，因为“嘿，它有效，不是吗”。不要误会我的意思，我总体上同意法学硕士是一个重要的里程碑。 但我想补充一点，特别是因为你的语气，对它有多大飞跃的看法很可能是个人能力的问题。 因此，您的热情可能更多地反映了您自己，而不是该工具。“对于那些自己从未做过这样的事情的人来说，结果可能看起来很棒......”我已经“这样做”了30多年，我认为这些LLM结果非常棒。如果一个人对工具、系统和协议有深入的、概念性的理解，并且如果一个人有能力仔细提供仔细、简洁的规范，那么这些LLM工具就像被授予了超能力。事实上......我的下一个项目是一个完全本地化、太阳能供电、自托管的LLM工具。工具链。我不仅打算不再在没有这些工具的情况下工作，而且打算在没有它们产生的持续环境外部性的情况下这样做。> 如果一个人对工具、系统和协议有深入的、概念性的理解，并且如果一个人有能力仔细提供仔细、简洁的规范，那么大多数人已经没有能力做到这一点，他们对法学硕士的使用也变得越来越少。 “AI原住民”将彻底完蛋。嗯，不想向你透露，但是当你加载开放权重模型时，环境外部性已经发生了但你确实计划继续工作，而不是让人工智能将自己引导成机器神，而是为人类做所有可能的工作。> 读到这里我笑了。 你真的尝试过吗？ 我最近试图让 Claude (Opus) 给我一个使用 Postfix 进行简单 SMTP 中继的设置。 我必须不断地努力不包含完全不必要的组件和配置，例如 Dovecot，或者只是忽略我的要求。 就连克劳德也只不过是一个半能干的教程答题者。即使我尽了最大努力，我也不能让克劳德变得这么无用。 我真的不知道这些说这种废话的人在输入提示时在做什么，但他们一定是完全难以理解的。 如果你输入任何合理数量的信息来说明你正在做什么、指导方针是什么等等，那么人工智能在这些任务上的能力绝对是令人瞠目结舌的。 如此不爱交流，一定是某种奇怪的荣誉徽章，以至于你无法让那些拼命地想阿谀奉承你所有绝妙想法的人工智能变得毫无用处。 这确实比简单地使用该技术更难做到，甚至还过得去。我主要认为人工智能将我的编码抽象提升了一个层次。 如果你想要任何模糊可维护的东西，而不是拉入大量不相关的库，它仍然需要保姆和验证，但现在我能够为〜50-100行任务提供清晰的规范，它会得到90%的正确率，而且这是一个足够小的块，我可以很好地验证它。尽管克劳德仍然足够慢，但我认为在我完成任务时可能会提高50%，但牺牲了深厚的知识和学习。 我担心我永远无法发展出高级工程师十年前所发展的相同技能和直觉，因为我外包了很多规划和思考。这可能有点苛刻，我（故意）迟到了这一切，因为我担心个人萎缩、安全和喂养修格斯，但根据我的指示，它会根据提示搅拌几个小时。 它按域使用我本地的碎布（它也帮助我构建和维护）。 最近的例子：它已经列在我设置头秤的清单上大约 5 年了。 我知道该怎么做，我已经阅读了文档，但一直推迟它，部分是因为 Tailscale 可以工作并且有慷慨的免费套餐，部分是因为我不想搁浅资产，我有些短视地强化了只接受来自我的 Tailnet 的 ssh。克劳德在 3 小时内完成了这一切，而我则在做其他事情，在需要密钥时提示“op”访问，并记录每次使用，而我的参与很少。 所有这些都包含了比我在周末会议中创建的更多的文档、回滚点和传播到适当抹布的“学习内容”。 它犯了错误（有些错误是我可能会犯的，比如区域设置），然后对其进行推理、更正和测试，所有这些都是透明的，所有 git 支持，所有都在我的 Mattermost 服务器上发布，所以我可以轻松地搜索它，就像一个非常详细的队友所做的那样。我不知道如何理解这一切，但让它在我的待办事项中搅动已经让人大开眼界。是的，也不是... 我担任开发人员已有 20 年了，大部分时间都没有花在编写代码上。 我的大部分时间都花在监控生产、参加会议、做设计工作、进行代码/规范审查、为基础设施/IT 创建票证来部署东西......所以，是的，我喜欢人工智能，它确实对我的个人项目有帮助。 但我的实际工作呢？ 稍微，我可以在会议期间在后台做一些不错的公关工作。 我的编码效率可能会提高 20%？ 主要是因为我做了更多容易实现的公关工作，例如错误修复和重构。 困难的事情仍然需要思考。 但这还不到我一半的时间。 这很有趣，但并不能改变世界。> 我只是不明白为什么有人会认为我们回到了 LLM 之前的世界。这不是问题，问题是开发者每拯救一个小时，社会就会在虚假数据、虚假错误报告、自动诈骗、不必要的复杂电子邮件等方面损失 10 个小时。因为让一个奴隶做你所说的任何事情也可以实现一些非常出色的工作流程，但这并不能阻止它在道德和经济上过于昂贵，无法变得无处不在和可持续。 法学硕士的使用当然不涉及智慧生物，因此不会像奴隶制那样受到道德谴责，但实际上，它可能更像是一种财务困境。问题不在于能力（而且从来都不是）。 问题是可靠性。这是假设你确实需要使用法学硕士来完成这些类型的任务。 正如GP所说，正如你已经证明的那样，大多数人都试图将LLM用作锤子来处理所有看起来像钉子的东西，而不首先验证它是否是螺钉或其他东西。1. 不过，您所说的一切更多的是关于 IT 革命。 IT 比现实生活中的其他事物更容易测试。2． 奇点和真正强大的自动化之间的差距是巨大的。 基本上是在 100 万和无穷大之间，或者更好的是，100 万和 10 亿之间的差异大约是 10 亿。#1 对我来说太疯狂以至于人们难以理解。 没有人说我们要回到LLM之前的世界；只是这个世界与以前并没有什么不同。 大多数人的生活都差不多。 他们的电费更高，但这与由于新的石油战争而导致天然气价格开始上涨的情况并没有什么不同。 政府有一些新的方法来监视和瞄准武器，但这对大多数人来说相当抽象（而且他们以前在做这些事情时并不遇到困难）。 一些新的科技公司突然出现并拥有大量资金，而一些现有的公司则资金较少，但科技行业之外的人在对股价的关注程度方面还不是一个巨大的异常值，没有人真正关心这一点。如果这是奇点，请认为我非常不知所措。 我的工作发生了变化，但对于世界其他地方来说，情况却大同小异。LLM 非常擅长编程。 这很清楚。 2030 年的软件工程将与 2020 年截然不同。 现在人们也有机会使用代码来解决以前不经济的问题，因为雇用程序员的成本很高。人工智能在所有领域都会以相同的方式运作吗？ 这就是当前的叙述。是的，这是一个有用的工具。 这与奇点、递归 ASI、全自动经济或任何被炒作的社会彻底变革有什么关系？没有一场革命会像计划或宣传的那样发生革命性的变化。 相反，一旦触发，它大多是无计划的。 它是人类行为的产物，而不是人类设计的产物。个人将在他们认为适合自己工作的情况下使用法学硕士。 他们会犯很多错误，因为他们没有清楚地看到自己的工作，也因为他们没有真正理解别人关心的结果。 当然，靠嘴赚钱的高管会无休止地张开嘴，即使他们不懂技术，因为他们从未真正使用过电子邮件、短信和电话之外的任何技术。 等等。没有人知道法学硕士最终将如何摆脱困境。 但即使是克劳德使用了五分钟，也很难想象所有的白领工作都会像过去一样继续下去。即使在我十几岁的时候，奇点对我来说也没有意义。 为什么人工智能能力会突然出现天文数字的提升？ 它仍然会在我们（人类）设计的硬件和软件上运行。 即使人工智能达到了与人类大脑相当的水平，它仍然需要将效率提高几个数量级才能达到与人类社会产出相当的水平。 只有这样，它才能开始超越人类正在做出的改进。即使人工智能奇点是可能的，加速也需要很长时间。 可能是一辈子。奇点是人工智能可以比人类理解的速度更快地改进自身的转折点，因此软件至少不再是人类设计的。它需要一定程度的自我驱动意图并访问自己的代码，而法学硕士无法独自完成。 不过，它们可能是这样一个未来系统的组成部分，我们只是还不知道。 即便如此，也可能需要相当长的时间才能达到这个临界点。 GP 错了，他说得好像我们已经达到了目标一样。关于奇点，我一直不明白的是，它只是一些著名人士随着时间的推移提出的一个概念。 人工智能有很多不同的发展方式，有些是我们无法预见的。我认为人们认为事情会比他们做的更快。 人工智能革命可能有点像工业革命，从 1712 年纽科门的蒸汽机到大规模生产的汽车等，工业革命花了几个世纪的时间。 对于人工智能，图灵在 1950 年提出了他的测试，花了大约 75 年才达到看起来通过的程度。 给它时间。对了，还有两条平行的轨道。 首先是“每一个裂缝和缝隙”部分——“用人工智能总结”、“用人工智能重写”、“帮我写”、“用人工智能分析”，基本上无用的功能以融入人工智能的名义散布在各处，同时惹恼了每个人。第二个是人工智能竞赛/存在风险/假装我们是奥本海默赛道，我认为这是最贪婪的，基本上假装LLM聊天机器人是某种超级武器和相应的角色扮演。没有这意味着它们很有用，人工智能编码显然是一种有价值的工具（尽管仍有很多问题需要解决）。 但不幸的是，正如你所说，人们没有重新设定期望，而是想玩终结者或神经机器或其他什么（我确信有更多相关的科幻小说），从而掩盖了其有用性。> 我们大多数人在某种程度上都相信人工智能将彻底改变我们的社会。认真的吗？ 也许我只是老了，但经历了 20 世纪 90 年代的互联网炒作、2000 年代的 XML 炒作和 2010 年代的云炒作，这是一个完全可以预测的围绕人工智能的炒作周期。 这并不意味着人工智能完全无用。 它将影响世界，但不会像万金油推销员声称的那样。 这与互联网非常相似。 最好的泡沫建立在真理的核心之上，然后被吹得不成比例，并包裹在层层蛇油制造中。>我个人迫不及待地希望这一切结束，并让每个人都集体回去等待下一张应该解决我们所有问题的金票。所以，你也决心爱上下一张，不是吗？狂热可以追溯到很久以前。1840年代英国铁路狂热的历史值得研究，因为它惊人地相似，都有股票泡沫、欺诈者和投机者、巨大的炒作、残酷的竞争、董事会的阴谋和戏剧、在错误的地方绵延数英里的不必要的基础设施，以及最终变得有用但可以更智能地开发的技术。狂热对于相对年轻的人工智能领域来说是如此的基础，以至于他们创造了“人工智能冬天”这个词。 我们应该很快为第三个（我认为）人工智能冬天做好准备当当前的狂热结束时，这将更像是一个技术/软件的冬天，而不仅仅是人工智能。 我预计公众对科技公司和政治将完全不信任，以推动严格的监管铁路行业纠正后的削减的悖论是，他们拆除的每一条轨道现在或多或少都是有用的。 例如，比钦削减对东萨塞克斯造成的损害是毁灭性的且无法修复。人类需要狂热来开发新技术。 这只是人类状况的一部分。因为它的愚蠢而对风车倾斜只是我试图克服的事情，而不是在个人层面上关心。 无论如何我都无能为力！铁路现在主要是国家资助的基础设施。 在我看来，法学硕士也会发生同样的情况，这似乎是合理的。> 经历过 20 世纪 90 年代的网络炒作虽然没有经历过这个，但我实际上很好奇它与当前的炒作周期有多么相似。 是否有一种普遍的感觉，类似奇点的东西正在逼近？我不认为也不会声称人工智能完全无用，我个人在每个工作日至少使用它几个小时，并觉得它显着提高了我的生产力。 我的观点是，我们从人工智能中看到的好处并不是我们所期望的，而且大多数人似乎还没有意识到期望与现实之间的差异。 我经常听到朋友们谈论即将到来的后稀缺未来，就像 Ian M. 的文化思想一样。 银行，而这种伪宗教叙事似乎是人工智能巨头故意推销的。 我实际上看到的东西更类似于《瑞克和莫蒂》中的米塞克斯先生。> 所以，你也注定会爱上下一个，是吗？哈哈，不。 关键是，我在炒作周期之间的漫长冬天看到了比任何炒作周期更好的事情。如果您阅读 1998 年至 1999 年间的媒体，您会发现一切都在发生变化。 现在每个企业都需要上网。 任何不投资互联网的企业都将被屠杀。 实体店被视为资产负债表上的负面因素。 短短几年内，所有商业都将成为电子商务。
